sol_sql:
/* 
Intent: Analyze signal quality (SNQI) with atmospheric conditions using window functions
Knowledge Used: SNQI (id:0)
Advanced Features: Window functions, conditional aggregation
*/
WITH signal_quality AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate SNQI for all signals with their observation conditions
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm) AS SNQI,
        o.WeathProfile
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN Telescopes t ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    JOIN Observatories o ON t.ObservStation = o.ObservStation
)
-- Step 2: Rank signals by quality within weather profiles
SELECT 
    WeathProfile,
    AVG(SNQI) AS avg_snqi,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY SNQI) AS median_snqi,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE SNQI > 0) AS analyzable_signals
FROM signal_quality
GROUP BY WeathProfile
ORDER BY avg_snqi DESC;

/*
Intent: Analyze distribution of technosignature probabilities using statistical functions
Knowledge Used: TOLS (id:3)
Advanced Features: Statistical functions, CASE expressions
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate TOLS for all signals with probability data
SELECT 
    -- Step 2: Bin the TOLS values into categories
    CASE 
        WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.25 THEN 'Low'
        WHEN p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) < 0.75 THEN 'Medium'
        ELSE 'High'
    END AS tol_category,
    -- Step 3: Calculate statistics for each category
    COUNT(*) AS signal_count,
    AVG(s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000)) AS avg_bfr,
    STDDEV(p.AnomScore) AS anomaly_stddev
FROM Signals s
JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
GROUP BY tol_category;

/*
Intent: Quantify lunar interference impact using LIF calculation
Knowledge Used: LIF (id:9)
Advanced Features: Date functions, lateral join
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate LIF for all observations with lunar data
SELECT 
    o.ObservStation,
    -- Step 2: Calculate average LIF by moon phase
    o.LunarStage,
    AVG((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS avg_lif,
    -- Step 3: Count high interference events
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE (1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency) > 0.5) AS high_interf_count
FROM Observatories o
GROUP BY o.ObservStation, o.LunarStage
ORDER BY avg_lif DESC;

/*
Intent: Evaluate detection efficiency for potential technosignatures
Knowledge Used: Technosignature (id:10), TOLS (id:3)
Advanced Features: Common Table Expressions, complex filtering
*/
WITH tech_signals AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        s.TelescRef,
        p.TechSigProb * (1 - p.NatSrcProb) * p.SigUnique * (0.5 + p.AnomScore/10) AS TOLS,
        s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000) AS BFR
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    WHERE p.TechSigProb > 0.7 
      AND p.NatSrcProb < 0.3 
      AND p.ArtSrcProb < 50
      AND s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000) < 0.001
)
SELECT 
    t.ObservStation,
    COUNT(*) AS potential_tech_signals,
    AVG(ts.TOLS) AS avg_tols,
    AVG(ts.BFR) AS avg_bfr,
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tech_signals) AS percentage_of_total
FROM tech_signals ts
JOIN Telescopes t ON ts.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
GROUP BY t.ObservStation
ORDER BY potential_tech_signals DESC;

/*
Intent: Evaluate effectiveness of different modulation types
Knowledge Used: MCS (id:30)
Advanced Features: JSON functions, advanced aggregation
*/
SELECT 
    s.ModType,
    COUNT(*) AS signal_count,
    AVG(s.ModIndex * (1 + (1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz*1000)) * 
        s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000)) *
        CASE 
            WHEN s.ModType = 'AM' THEN 2
            WHEN s.ModType = 'FM' THEN 1.5
            ELSE 1
        END) AS avg_mcs,
    AVG(s.SnrRatio) AS avg_snr,
    JSON_OBJECT_AGG(
        s.SignalRegistry,
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'mcs', s.ModIndex * (1 + (1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz*1000)) * 
                   s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000)) *
                   CASE 
                       WHEN s.ModType = 'AM' THEN 2
                       WHEN s.ModType = 'FM' THEN 1.5
                       ELSE 1
                   END,
            'snr', s.SnrRatio
        )
    ) AS signal_details
FROM Signals s
WHERE s.ModType IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY s.ModType
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

/*
Intent: Optimize research priorities based on multiple factors
Knowledge Used: RPI (id:8), CCS (id:36)
Advanced Features: Complex calculations, window functions
*/
WITH priority_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        (p.TechSigProb * 4 + p.BioSigProb/100 + p.SigUnique * 2 + p.AnomScore/2) * 
            (1 - p.FalsePosProb) AS RPI,
        (1 - p.FalsePosProb) * d.DecodeConf * 
            (CASE WHEN s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm) > 0 
                  THEN (s.SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(s.NoiseFloorDbm))/10 + 0.5 
                  ELSE 0.1 END) AS CCS_approx,
        o.ObservStation
    FROM Signals s
    JOIN SignalProbabilities p ON s.SignalRegistry = p.SignalRef
    JOIN SignalDecoding d ON s.SignalRegistry = d.SignalRef
    JOIN Telescopes t ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    JOIN Observatories o ON t.ObservStation = o.ObservStation
)
SELECT 
    ObservStation,
    COUNT(*) AS signal_count,
    AVG(RPI) AS avg_rpi,
    AVG(CCS_approx) AS avg_ccs,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE RPI > 3) AS high_priority_signals,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE CCS_approx > 0.8) AS high_confidence_signals,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE RPI > 3 AND CCS_approx > 0.8) AS high_priority_high_confidence
FROM priority_calc
GROUP BY ObservStation
ORDER BY high_priority_high_confidence DESC;

-- This query evaluates optimal observatory conditions using Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI)
-- and generates a JSON report of environmental factors using JSON aggregation functions.
-- Using KB knowledge: Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI) [id: 1], Optimal Observing Window (OOW) [id: 13]

-- Step 1: Calculate AOI for each observatory and determine if conditions meet Optimal Observing Window criteria
WITH observatory_conditions AS (
    SELECT 
        o.ObservStation,
        o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs) AS aoi,  -- AOI formula from KB
        o.LunarStage,
        o.LunarDistDeg,
        o.SolarStatus,
        -- Check if conditions meet OOW criteria
        CASE WHEN 
            o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs) > 0.85 AND
            (o.LunarStage = 'New' OR o.LunarStage = 'First Quarter') AND
            o.LunarDistDeg > 45 AND
            (o.SolarStatus = 'Low' OR o.SolarStatus = 'Moderate')
        THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_optimal_window
    FROM 
        Observatories o
)
-- Step 2: Generate JSON report grouping observatories by optimal condition status
SELECT 
    is_optimal_window,
    COUNT(*) AS station_count,
    ROUND(AVG(aoi)::numeric, 3) AS avg_aoi,
    -- Use JSON aggregation to create detailed environmental factors report
    jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object(
        'station', ObservStation,
        'aoi', ROUND(aoi::numeric, 3),
        'lunar_factors', jsonb_build_object(
            'stage', LunarStage,
            'distance', LunarDistDeg
        ),
        'solar_status', SolarStatus
    )) AS observatory_details
FROM 
    observatory_conditions
GROUP BY 
    is_optimal_window;

/* 
-- Intent: Find signals matching Narrowband Technological Marker criteria with spectrum stability
-- Uses KB item 15 (NTM) and advanced LATERAL JOIN for computational efficiency
*/
-- Step 1: Calculate BFR using a lateral join to improve query performance
SELECT 
    s.SignalRegistry,
    s.CenterFreqMhz,
    s.FreqDriftHzs,
    -- Calculate BFR using lateral join results
    nbcalc.BFR,
    -- Using CASE for categorization based on NTM criteria
    CASE 
        WHEN nbcalc.BFR < 0.0001 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 0.1 
        THEN 'Strong NTM'
        WHEN nbcalc.BFR < 0.0005 AND s.FreqDriftHzs < 0.5 
        THEN 'Moderate NTM'
        ELSE 'Not NTM'
    END AS ntm_classification
FROM Signals s
-- Step 2: Use LATERAL JOIN for efficient calculation of BFR
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT (s.BwHz/(s.CenterFreqMhz * 1000000)) AS BFR
) AS nbcalc
-- Step 3: Apply primary NTM criteria from KB item 15
WHERE 
    nbcalc.BFR < 0.001 AND
    s.FreqDriftHzs < 1.0;

-- Intent: Evaluate combined observatory conditions and equipment status for observation quality
-- Uses KB item 16 (OCL) and 32 (OQF), with advanced array functions
-- Step 1: Calculate observation quality metrics using various observatory and telescope parameters
WITH quality_assessment AS (
    SELECT 
        o.ObservStation,
        t.TelescRegistry,
        o.AtmosTransparency,
        o.HumidityRate,
        o.WindSpeedMs,
        o.LunarDistDeg,
        t.EquipStatus,
        t.CalibrStatus,
        t.PointAccArc,
        -- Calculate AOI from KB item 1
        (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS AOI,
        -- Calculate LIF from KB item 9
        ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS LIF,
        -- Store equipment issues in array for analysis
        ARRAY[
            CASE WHEN t.EquipStatus != 'Degraded' THEN t.EquipStatus ELSE NULL END,
            CASE WHEN t.CalibrStatus != 'Overdue' THEN t.CalibrStatus ELSE NULL END,
            CASE WHEN t.CoolSysStatus != 'Warning' THEN t.CoolSysStatus ELSE NULL END
        ] AS equipment_issues
    FROM Observatories o
    INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
)
-- Step 2: Apply OCL criteria and calculate OQF using conditional logic
SELECT 
    qa.ObservStation,
    qa.TelescRegistry,
    -- Calculate OQF based on KB item 32
    (qa.AOI * (1 - qa.LIF) * (CASE WHEN qa.PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2/qa.PointAccArc END)) AS OQF,
    -- Display equipment issues using array functions
    ARRAY_TO_STRING(ARRAY_REMOVE(qa.equipment_issues, NULL), ', ') AS identified_issues,
    -- Count equipment issues using array functions
    CARDINALITY(ARRAY_REMOVE(qa.equipment_issues, NULL)) AS issue_count,
    -- Apply OCL classification criteria from KB item 16
    CASE 
        WHEN qa.AOI > 0.8 AND qa.EquipStatus = 'Operational' AND qa.CalibrStatus = 'Current' 
        THEN 'High'
        WHEN qa.AOI BETWEEN 0.5 AND 0.8 
        THEN 'Medium'
        ELSE 'Low'
    END AS ocl_classification
FROM quality_assessment qa
-- Step 3: Order by calculated quality for prioritization
ORDER BY OQF DESC;

-- Intent: Identify signals showing evidence of coherent information patterns based on stability
-- Uses KB item 7 (SSM) and 11 (CIP), with advanced string aggregation
-- Step 1: Calculate Signal Stability Metric (SSM) using mathematical operations
WITH stability_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        s.SignalRegistry,
        s.SignalClass,
        s.FreqDriftHzs,
        s.FreqMhz,
        s.SigDurSec,
        s.DoppShiftHz,
        s.ModType,
        s.ModIndex,
        sd.SigIntegrity,
        sd.FreqStab,
        sd.PhaseStab,
        sc.EntropyVal,
        -- Calculate SSM per KB item 7
        ((1 - ABS(s.FreqDriftHzs)/(s.FreqMhz * 1000)) * 
         (s.SigDurSec/(1 + s.DoppShiftHz/1000))) AS SSM
    FROM Signals s
    INNER JOIN SignalDynamics sd ON s.SignalRegistry = sd.SignalRef
    INNER JOIN SignalClassification sc ON s.SignalRegistry = sc.SignalRef
)
-- Step 2: Analyze coherent information patterns with string aggregation
SELECT 
    sa.SignalRegistry,
    sa.SignalClass,
    ROUND(sa.SSM::numeric, 3) AS SSM,
    -- Aggregate stability factors using string_agg
    string_agg(
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute = 'FreqStab' THEN 'Frequency: ' || value
            WHEN attribute = 'PhaseStab' THEN 'Phase: ' || value
            WHEN attribute = 'SigIntegrity' THEN 'Integrity: ' || value
        END,
        ', ' ORDER BY attribute
    ) AS stability_factors,
    -- Evaluate if signal meets CIP criteria from KB item 11
    CASE 
        WHEN sa.SSM > 0.8 AND 
             sa.EntropyVal BETWEEN 0.4 AND 0.8 AND
             sa.ModIndex > 0.5
        THEN 'Coherent Information Pattern Detected'
        WHEN sa.SSM > 0.6 AND sa.EntropyVal BETWEEN 0.3 AND 0.9
        THEN 'Potential Information Pattern'
        ELSE 'No Clear Pattern'
    END AS pattern_assessment
FROM stability_analysis sa
-- Step 3: Use lateral join with unnest to transform stability attributes into rows for aggregation
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT unnest(ARRAY['FreqStab', 'PhaseStab', 'SigIntegrity']) AS attribute,
           unnest(ARRAY[sa.FreqStab, sa.PhaseStab, sa.SigIntegrity]) AS value
) AS stability_attrs
GROUP BY 
    sa.SignalRegistry, sa.SignalClass, sa.SSM, sa.EntropyVal, sa.ModType, sa.ModIndex;

-- Update Signals to flag low-quality detections.
-- Intent: Mark signals with a computed Signal-to-Noise Quality Indicator (SNQI) below 0 by setting their SignalClass to 'Weak'.
-- Step 1: Compute SNQI inline using the expression: SNQI = SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(NoiseFloorDbm).
-- Step 2: Use the WHERE clause to filter records with SNQI < 0.
-- Knowledge Used: "Signal-to-Noise Quality Indicator (SNQI)" [KB id:0]
UPDATE Signals
SET SignalClass = 'Weak'
WHERE (SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(NoiseFloorDbm)) < 0;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # Define the verification SQL to check that no row with SNQI < 0 remains unflagged.
    verification_sql = (
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Signals "
        "WHERE (SnrRatio - 0.1 * ABS(NoiseFloorDbm)) < 0 "
        "AND SignalClass <> 'Weak';"
    )
    
    # Execute the verification SELECT query.
    pred_result, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)

    # Expect pred_result to be a list with one tuple containing the count.
    # Use assert to check that the count is zero.
    # For example, if pred_result = [(0,)], then pred_result[0][0] should be 0.
    assert pred_result[0][0] == 0, (
        f"Predicted SQL update did not update correctly: "
        f"{pred_result[0][0]} rows still have SNQI < 0 without 'Weak' flag."
    )

sol_sql:
-- Create a function to calculate Detection Instrument Sensitivity Factor (DISF).
-- Intent: Define a function that computes DISF using telescope environmental data and then test it.
-- Step 1: Create the function "calculate_disf" using PL/pgSQL.
--         Inputs: AirTempC, AtmosTransparency, HumidityRate, LunarDistDeg.
--         Calculation: DISF = (10 - |AirTempC - 15|/10) * AtmosTransparency * (1 - HumidityRate/200) * ((100 - LunarDistDeg)/100)
-- Knowledge Used:
--   "Detection Instrument Sensitivity Factor (DISF)" [KB id:5] and advanced procedural language features.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_disf(
    p_airtemp NUMERIC, 
    p_trans NUMERIC, 
    p_humidity NUMERIC, 
    p_lunar_deg NUMERIC
) RETURNS NUMERIC AS $$
DECLARE
    v_disf NUMERIC;
BEGIN
    -- Calculate the DISF based on the given formula.
    v_disf := (10 - ABS(p_airtemp - 15)/10) * p_trans * (1 - p_humidity/200) * ((100 - p_lunar_deg)/100);
    RETURN v_disf;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # The expected DISF value computed for inputs: AirTempC = 20, p_trans = 0.95, p_humidity = 45, p_lunar_deg = 60.
    # Calculation:
    #   factor1 = 10 - ABS(20 - 15)/10 = 10 - 5/10 = 10 - 0.5 = 9.5
    #   factor2 = 0.95
    #   factor3 = 1 - 45/200 = 1 - 0.225 = 0.775
    #   factor4 = (100 - 60)/100 = 40/100 = 0.4
    # Expected DISF = 9.5 * 0.95 * 0.775 * 0.4 ≈ 2.79775
    expected_disf = 2.79775
    tolerance = 0.00001  # Allowable floating-point error
    
    # Execute a SELECT to call calculate_disf with given parameters.
    pred_result, _, _ = execute_queries("SELECT calculate_disf(20, 0.95, 45, 60);", db_name, conn)
    # Expecting pred_result to be a list with one tuple containing the computed value.
    predicted_value = float(pred_result[0][0])
    
    # Step 3: Assert that the predicted value matches the expected DISF.
    assert abs(predicted_value - expected_disf) < tolerance, (
        f"Predicted DISF value {predicted_value} differs from expected {expected_disf}"
    )

sol_sql:
-- Create a view to show observation quality factors.
-- Intent: Join Observatories, Telescopes, and Signals to compute an "Observation Quality Factor (OQF)"
--         that is based on AOI, LIF, and pointing accuracy.
--
-- Step 1: Use a CTE to join three tables using INNER JOIN.
-- Step 2: Compute AOI = AtmosTransparency * (1 - HumidityRate/100) * (1 - 0.02 * WindSpeedMs).
-- Step 3: Compute LIF = (1 - LunarDistDeg/180) * (1 - AtmosTransparency).
-- Step 4: Compute OQF = AOI * (1 - LIF) * (CASE WHEN PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2/PointAccArc END).
--
-- Advanced features used: CASE expressions, arithmetic operations, and a CTE for cleaner joins.
--
-- Knowledge Used:
--   "Atmospheric Observability Index (AOI)" [KB id:1],
--   "Lunar Interference Factor (LIF)" [KB id:9],
--   and "Observation Quality Factor (OQF)" [KB id:32].
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW vw_observation_quality AS
WITH calc AS (
  SELECT
    o.ObservStation,
    t.TelescRegistry,
    s.SignalRegistry,
    o.AtmosTransparency,
    o.HumidityRate,
    o.WindSpeedMs,
    o.LunarDistDeg,
    t.PointAccArc,
    -- Compute AOI based on environmental conditions.
    (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100.0) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS aoi,
    -- Compute LIF based on lunar distance and transparency.
    ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS lif
  FROM Observatories o
  INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
  INNER JOIN Signals s ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
)
SELECT
  ObservStation,
  TelescRegistry,
  SignalRegistry,
  aoi,
  lif,
  aoi * (1 - lif) * (CASE WHEN PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2.0/PointAccArc END) AS oqf
FROM calc;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    tolerance = 0.0001  # Allowable floating point difference
    # Define a verification query that joins the view with base tables to compute expected oqf.
    verification_sql = """
    SELECT 
        v.oqf AS view_oqf,
        (calc.aoi * (1 - calc.lif) * 
            (CASE WHEN calc.PointAccArc < 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2.0/calc.PointAccArc END)
        ) AS expected_oqf
    FROM vw_observation_quality v
    JOIN (
        SELECT
            o.ObservStation,
            t.TelescRegistry,
            s.SignalRegistry,
            (o.AtmosTransparency * (1 - o.HumidityRate/100.0) * (1 - 0.02 * o.WindSpeedMs)) AS aoi,
            ((1 - o.LunarDistDeg/180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency)) AS lif,
            t.PointAccArc
        FROM Observatories o
        INNER JOIN Telescopes t ON o.ObservStation = t.ObservStation
        INNER JOIN Signals s ON s.TelescRef = t.TelescRegistry
    ) calc
    ON v.ObservStation = calc.ObservStation
    AND v.TelescRegistry = calc.TelescRegistry
    AND v.SignalRegistry = calc.SignalRegistry;
    """
    # Execute verification query.
    pred_verification_result, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)
    # For each returned row, assert that view_oqf is within tolerance of expected_oqf.
    for row in pred_verification_result:
        view_oqf = float(row[0])
        expected_oqf = float(row[1])
        assert abs(view_oqf - expected_oqf) < tolerance, (
            f"Predicted view error: For row with ObservStation/TelescRegistry/SignalRegistry, "
            f"view oqf ({view_oqf}) does not match expected oqf ({expected_oqf}) within tolerance."
        )

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # Insert test data into the Signals table and verify the trigger logic.
    insert_sql = """
    INSERT INTO Signals (SignalRegistry, SnrRatio, NoiseFloorDbm, TelescRef)
    VALUES 
        ('S001', 10, -50, 'T4621'),  -- SNQI = 10 - 0.1 * |-50| = 5 (IsAnalyzable = TRUE)
        ('S002', 3, -40, 'T4621');   -- SNQI = 3 - 0.1 * |-40| = -1 (IsAnalyzable = FALSE)
    """
    execute_queries(insert_sql, db_name, conn)
    verification_sql = """
    SELECT SignalRegistry, IsAnalyzable
    FROM Signals
    WHERE SignalRegistry IN ('S001', 'S002')
    ORDER BY SignalRegistry;
    """
    pred_result, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)
    # Expected results based on SNQI computation.
    expected_result = [
        ('S001', True),  # SNQI > 0
        ('S002', False)  # SNQI <= 0
    ]
    # Assert the predicted results match the expected results.
    for actual, expected in zip(pred_result, expected_result):
        actual_registry, actual_flag = actual
        expected_registry, expected_flag = expected
        assert actual_registry.strip() == expected_registry.strip() and actual_flag == expected_flag, (
            f"Predicted SQL trigger test failed: Expected {expected_result}, got {pred_result}"
        )

sol_sql:
-- Update telescope equipment status based on Lunar Interference Factor (LIF).
-- Step 1: Dynamically compute LIF using:
--         LIF = (1 - LunarDistDeg/180) × (1 - AtmosTransparency).
--         Values above 0.5 suggest significant lunar contamination.
-- Step 2: Update telescope equipment status to 'Under Lunar Interference' for high LIF values.
-- Step 3: Use a subquery to JOIN `Telescopes` with `Observatories` for the necessary data.

-- Knowledge Used:
--   - "Lunar Interference Factor (LIF)" [KB id:9]
--   - Advanced SQL: Subqueries with JOIN and conditional updates.

UPDATE Telescopes
SET EquipStatus = 'Under Lunar Interference'
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM Observatories o
    WHERE o.ObservStation = Telescopes.ObservStation
      AND (1 - o.LunarDistDeg / 180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency) > 0.5
);
-- Result: Updates telescopes where lunar interference is deemed significant.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # Observatories with LIF > 0.5 are identified, and their linked telescopes are checked.
    verification_sql = """
    SELECT COUNT(*)
    FROM Telescopes t
    JOIN Observatories o ON t.ObservStation = o.ObservStation
    WHERE (1 - o.LunarDistDeg / 180.0) * (1 - o.AtmosTransparency) > 0.5
    AND t.EquipStatus <> 'Under Lunar Interference';
    """

    # Execute the verification SELECT query.
    pred_result, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)

    # Expect pred_result to be a list with one tuple containing the count.
    # Use assert to check that the count is zero.
    # For example, if pred_result = [(0,)], then pred_result[0][0] should be 0.
    assert pred_result[0][0] == 0, (
        f"Predicted SQL update did not update correctly: "
        f"{pred_result[0][0]} telescopes linked to high LIF observatories do not have 'Under Lunar Interference' status."
    )

sol_sql:
/*
 * SQL Intent: Calculate the average Scan Quality Score (SQS) for each site to evaluate scan quality.
 * Knowledge Used: #3 (Scan Quality Score - SQS)
 * Advanced Functions: LOG(), POWER(), AVG(), GROUP BY
 */

SELECT
    s.zoneref AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    -- Step 1: Calculate the average SQS for each site and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        AVG(
            POWER(10.0 / (LOG(10, spc.scanresolmm * 1000.0) / LOG(10, spc.pointdense)) * 5.0, 1.5) *
            (spc.coverpct / 100.0) *
            POWER(1.0 - spc.noisedb / 30.0, 2)
        ),
        2
    ) AS avg_sqs
FROM scans s
JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
JOIN sites si ON s.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
GROUP BY s.zoneref, si.zonelabel
ORDER BY avg_sqs DESC;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Identify sites in "Degradation Risk Zones" based on preservation status and structural stability.
 * Knowledge Used: #14 (Degradation Risk Zone), #26, #52
 * Advanced Functions: CASE, String Matching (IN)
 */

SELECT
    si.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    sc.structstate AS structural_state,
    si.presstat AS preservation_status,
    -- Step 1: Classify sites into degradation risk zones
    CASE
        WHEN si.presstat IN ('Poor', 'Critical') AND sc.structstate <> 'Stable' THEN 'Degradation Risk Zone'
        ELSE 'Not in Risk Zone'
    END AS risk_zone
FROM sites si
LEFT JOIN scanconservation sc ON si.zoneregistry = sc.zoneref;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Calculate the Environmental Suitability Index (ESI) for each site based on temperature, humidity, and light conditions.
 * Knowledge Used: #7 (Environmental Suitability Index - ESI)
 * Advanced Functions: ABS(), POWER(), AVG(), GROUP BY
 */

SELECT
    se.zoneref AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    -- Step 1: Calculate the average ESI for each site and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        AVG(
            100.0 - 2.5 * ABS(se.ambictemp - 20.0) -
            POWER(ABS((se.humepct - 50.0) / 2.0), 1.5) -
            600.0 / (se.illumelux + 100.0)
        ),
        2
    ) AS avg_esi
FROM scanenvironment se
JOIN sites si ON se.zoneref = si.zoneregistry
GROUP BY se.zoneref, si.zonelabel;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Analyze registration accuracy and classify confidence levels based on RAR and reference markers.
 * Knowledge Used: #33 (Registration Accuracy Ratio - RAR), #44 (Registration Confidence Level)
 * Advanced Functions: SQRT(), CASE, Window Function (RANK())
 */

SELECT
    sr.logregistry AS registration_id,
    sr.arcref AS project_id,
    sr.logaccumm AS registration_accuracy_mm,
    sr.errvalmm AS error_value_mm,
    -- Step 1: Calculate RAR and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        (spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))),
        2
    ) AS rar,
    sr.logmethod,
    -- Step 2: Classify confidence level
    CASE
        WHEN (spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))) > 1.5
             AND sr.refmark LIKE '%Target%' THEN 'High Confidence'
        WHEN (spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))) BETWEEN 1.0 AND 1.5 THEN 'Medium Confidence'
        ELSE 'Low Confidence'
    END AS confidence_level
FROM scanregistration sr
JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON sr.arcref = spc.arcref;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Evaluate the Digital Preservation Quality (DPQ) for each site based on ADC, MFS, RAR, and SCE.
 * Knowledge Used: #9 (ADC), #6 (MFS), #33 (RAR), #1 (SCE), #38 (DPQ)
 * Advanced Functions: SQRT(), ROUND(), Aggregation, JOIN
 */

SELECT
    si.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    si.zonelabel AS site_name,
    -- Step 1: Calculate DPQ based on weighted components and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        CAST(
            (0.3 * COALESCE(adc.adc, 0)) +
            (0.3 * COALESCE(mfs.mfs, 0)) +
            (0.2 * COALESCE(rar.rar, 0)) +
            (0.2 * COALESCE(sce.sce, 0)) -
            (2 * SQRT(COALESCE(err.error_ratio, 0)))
        AS NUMERIC), 
        2
    ) AS dpq
FROM sites si
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 2: Calculate Archaeological Documentation Completeness (ADC)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(
            (sqs.sqs * 0.4) + 
            (sm.mfs * 0.4) + 
            (spc.coverpct * (1.0 + spc.lappct / 100.0 * (1.0 - spc.coverpct / 100.0)) * 0.2) - 
            (5 * SQRT(spc.noisedb / 10.0))
        ) AS adc
    FROM scans s
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
    LEFT JOIN (
        -- SQS calculation
        SELECT 
            sc.arcref,
            AVG(POWER(10.0 / (LOG(10, sc.scanresolmm * 1000.0) / LOG(10, sc.pointdense)) * 5.0, 1.5) *
                (sc.coverpct * (1.0 + sc.lappct / 100.0 * (1.0 - sc.coverpct / 100.0)) / 100.0) *
                POWER(1.0 - sc.noisedb / 30.0, 2)) AS sqs
        FROM scanpointcloud sc
        GROUP BY sc.arcref
    ) sqs ON s.arcref = sqs.arcref
    LEFT JOIN (
        -- MFS calculation
        SELECT 
            sm.zoneref,
            AVG((sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * sm.facetresmm * sm.facetresmm) * 1000.0) * 
                (sm.texpix / (SQRT(sm.facetfaces) * sm.facetresmm) * 0.01 / 10.0) * 
                (1.0 + EXP(-sm.geomdeltamm))) AS mfs
        FROM scanmesh sm
        WHERE sm.facetverts > 0 AND sm.facetfaces > 0 AND sm.facetresmm > 0
        GROUP BY sm.zoneref
    ) sm ON s.zoneref = sm.zoneref
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) adc ON si.zoneregistry = adc.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 3: Calculate Model Fidelity Score (MFS)
    SELECT
        sm.zoneref,
        AVG((sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * sm.facetresmm * sm.facetresmm) * 1000.0) * 
            (sm.texpix / (SQRT(sm.facetfaces) * sm.facetresmm) * 0.01 / 10.0) * 
            (1.0 + EXP(-sm.geomdeltamm))) AS mfs
    FROM scanmesh sm
    WHERE sm.facetverts > 0 AND sm.facetfaces > 0 AND sm.facetresmm > 0
    GROUP BY sm.zoneref
) mfs ON si.zoneregistry = mfs.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 4: Calculate Registration Accuracy Ratio (RAR)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(spc.scanresolmm / (sr.logaccumm * SQRT(1.0 + sr.errvalmm / sr.logaccumm))) AS rar
    FROM scanregistration sr
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON sr.arcref = spc.arcref
    JOIN scans s ON sr.arcref = s.arcref
    WHERE sr.logaccumm > 0
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) rar ON si.zoneregistry = rar.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 5: Calculate Scan Coverage Effectiveness (SCE)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(spc.coverpct * (1.0 + spc.lappct / 100.0 * (1.0 - spc.coverpct / 100.0))) AS sce
    FROM scans s
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) sce ON si.zoneregistry = sce.zoneref
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Step 6: Calculate Error Ratio (ErrValMm/ScanResolMm)
    SELECT
        s.zoneref,
        AVG(sr.errvalmm / spc.scanresolmm) AS error_ratio
    FROM scanregistration sr
    JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON sr.arcref = spc.arcref
    JOIN scans s ON sr.arcref = s.arcref
    WHERE spc.scanresolmm > 0
    GROUP BY s.zoneref
) err ON si.zoneregistry = err.zoneref
ORDER BY dpq DESC;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Identify sites with High Fidelity Meshes and analyze mesh quality distribution
 * Knowledge Used: #13 (High Fidelity Mesh), #4 (Mesh Complexity Ratio - MCR) #53
 * Advanced Functions: CASE expressions, Window functions (RANK()), String functions
 */

WITH mesh_analysis AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate Mesh Complexity Ratio (MCR) and identify High Fidelity Meshes
    SELECT 
        sm.zoneref AS site_code,
        sm.facetregistry AS mesh_id,
        -- Calculate MCR based on Knowledge #4
        (sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2))) * 1000 AS mcr,
        sm.facetresmm,
        sm.geomdeltamm,
        -- Determine if mesh is high-fidelity based on Knowledge #13
        CASE 
            WHEN (sm.facetfaces / (sm.facetverts * POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2))) * 1000 > 5.0 
                 AND sm.facetresmm < 1.0 
                 AND sm.geomdeltamm < 0.5 THEN TRUE
            ELSE FALSE
        END AS is_high_fidelity
    FROM scanmesh sm
    WHERE sm.facetverts > 0 AND sm.facetfaces > 0 -- Avoid division by zero
)

-- Step 2: Aggregate mesh quality metrics by site and identify sites with high-fidelity meshes
SELECT 
    s.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    s.zonelabel AS site_name,
    COUNT(ma.mesh_id) AS total_meshes,
    -- Count high-fidelity meshes
    SUM(CASE WHEN ma.is_high_fidelity THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_fidelity_count,
    -- Calculate percentage of high-fidelity meshes
    ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN ma.is_high_fidelity THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::numeric / 
           NULLIF(COUNT(ma.mesh_id), 0) * 100), 2) AS high_fidelity_percentage,
    -- Calculate average MCR, resolution, and geometric accuracy
    ROUND(AVG(ma.mcr)::numeric, 2) AS avg_mcr,
    ROUND(AVG(ma.facetresmm)::numeric, 2) AS avg_mesh_resolution_mm,
    ROUND(AVG(ma.geomdeltamm)::numeric, 2) AS avg_geometric_accuracy_mm,
    -- Determine site's mesh quality classification
    CASE 
        WHEN COUNT(ma.mesh_id) = 0 THEN 'No Mesh Data'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN ma.is_high_fidelity THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Has High-Fidelity Meshes'
        ELSE 'Standard Mesh Quality'
    END AS mesh_quality_classification
FROM sites s
LEFT JOIN mesh_analysis ma ON s.zoneregistry = ma.site_code
GROUP BY s.zoneregistry, s.zonelabel
ORDER BY high_fidelity_percentage DESC, high_fidelity_count DESC;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Analyze environmental conditions at scanning sites to determine optimal scanning locations
 * Knowledge Used: #7 (Environmental Suitability Index - ESI), #15 (Optimal Scanning Conditions) #50
 * Advanced Functions: ABS(), POWER(), CASE expressions, Window functions (NTILE())
 */

-- Step 1: Calculate Environmental Suitability Index (ESI) for each environment record
WITH environment_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        se.zoneref AS site_code,
        se.ambictemp,
        se.humepct,
        se.illumelux,
        -- Calculate ESI based on Knowledge #7
        100 - 2.5 * ABS(se.ambictemp - 20) - 
        POWER(ABS((se.humepct - 50) / 2), 1.5) - 
        600 / (se.illumelux + 100) AS esi
    FROM scanenvironment se
)

-- Step 2: Aggregate environmental metrics by site and classify scanning conditions
SELECT 
    s.zoneregistry AS site_code,
    s.zonelabel AS site_name,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.ambictemp)::numeric, 1) AS avg_temperature_c,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.humepct)::numeric, 1) AS avg_humidity_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.illumelux)::numeric, 1) AS avg_illumination_lux,
    ROUND(AVG(ea.esi)::numeric, 1) AS avg_esi,
    -- Categorize sites by environmental suitability quartile
    NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY AVG(ea.esi)) AS esi_quartile,
    -- Classify scanning conditions based on Knowledge #15
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(ea.esi) > 85 THEN 'Optimal Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN AVG(ea.esi) > 70 THEN 'Good Scanning Conditions'
        WHEN AVG(ea.esi) > 50 THEN 'Acceptable Scanning Conditions'
        ELSE 'Challenging Scanning Conditions'
    END AS scanning_condition_class,
FROM sites s
JOIN environment_analysis ea ON s.zoneregistry = ea.site_code
GROUP BY s.zoneregistry, s.zonelabel
ORDER BY avg_esi DESC;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Analyze the efficiency of scan processing workflows to identify potential bottlenecks
 * Knowledge Used: #8 (Processing Efficiency Ratio - PER), #17 (Processing Bottleneck)
 * Advanced Functions: LOG(), GROUP BY, CASE expression
 */

-- Step 1: Calculate Processing Efficiency Ratio (PER) for each workflow based on Knowledge #8
SELECT 
    sp.flowsoft AS processing_software,
    sp.flowstage AS processing_stage,
    -- Calculate average processing metrics
    ROUND(AVG(sp.flowhrs)::numeric, 1) AS avg_processing_hours,
    ROUND(AVG(sp.proccpu)::numeric, 0) AS avg_cpu_usage_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(sp.procgpu)::numeric, 0) AS avg_gpu_usage_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(s.gbsize)::numeric, 1) AS avg_data_size_gb,
    -- Calculate PER based on Knowledge #8
    ROUND(
        AVG(
            s.gbsize * LOG(10, COALESCE(spc.totalpts, 1000000)) /
            (sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 200)
        )::numeric,
        1
    ) AS avg_per,
    -- Identify bottlenecks based on Knowledge #17
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(
            s.gbsize * LOG(10, COALESCE(spc.totalpts, 1000000)) /
            (sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 200)
        ) < 0.5 THEN 'Bottleneck Detected'
        ELSE 'Efficient'
    END AS efficiency_status,
    -- Count workflows
    COUNT(*) AS workflow_count
FROM scanprocessing sp
JOIN scans s ON sp.zoneref = s.zoneref
LEFT JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
WHERE sp.flowhrs > 0 AND (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) > 0
GROUP BY sp.flowsoft, sp.flowstage
ORDER BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(
            s.gbsize * LOG(10, COALESCE(spc.totalpts, 1000000)) /
            (sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 200)
        ) < 0.5 THEN 1
        ELSE 2
    END,
    avg_per;

/*
 * SQL Intent: Identify sites with high potential for feature extraction based on point cloud characteristics.
 * Knowledge Used: #2 (Point Cloud Density Ratio - PCDR), #32 (Feature Extraction Efficiency - FEE)
 * Advanced Functions: POWER(), NULLIF(), ROUND()
 * Difficulty: Normal
 */
WITH feature_potential AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate Point Cloud Density Ratio (PCDR) for each scan, using TotalPts, CloudDense, and AreaM2 from relevant tables.
    SELECT
        s.zoneref AS site_code,
        spc.totalpts,
        spc.clouddense,
        ss.aream2,
        CASE
            WHEN spc.clouddense > 0 AND ss.aream2 > 0 THEN spc.totalpts / (spc.clouddense * ss.aream2)
            ELSE 0  -- Handle cases where division by zero might occur
        END AS pcdr
    FROM sites si
    LEFT JOIN scans s ON si.zoneregistry = s.zoneref
    LEFT JOIN scanpointcloud spc ON s.arcref = spc.arcref
    LEFT JOIN scanspatial ss ON s.arcref = ss.arcref
),
extraction_metrics AS (
    -- Step 2: Calculate Feature Extraction Efficiency (FEE) using PCDR and feature counts from scanfeatures.
    SELECT
        fp.site_code,
        fp.pcdr,
        sf.traitcount,
        sf.articount,
        CASE
            WHEN fp.pcdr > 0 THEN (sf.traitcount + sf.articount) / (fp.pcdr * SQRT(fp.clouddense)) * 1000
            ELSE 0  -- Handle cases where division by zero might occur
        END AS fee
    FROM feature_potential fp
    JOIN scanfeatures sf ON fp.site_code = sf.zoneref
)
-- Step 3: Aggregate and present the results, calculating average PCDR and FEE for each site.
SELECT
    site_code,
    ROUND(AVG(pcdr)::numeric, 2) AS avg_pcdr,
    ROUND(AVG(fee)::numeric, 2) AS avg_fee
FROM extraction_metrics
GROUP BY site_code
ORDER BY avg_fee DESC;

/* Intent: Analyze processing efficiency and resource utilization
   Knowledge Used: #37 (Processing Resource Utilization), #51
   Advanced Features: Advanced aggregation, JSON functions, Window functions
*/

WITH resource_metrics AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate resource utilization metrics
    SELECT 
        sp.flowregistry,
        sp.equipref,
        sp.flowhrs,
        sp.proccpu,
        sp.procgpu,
        sp.remaingb,
        sm.facetfaces,
        sm.facetverts,
        -- Calculate PRU
        sp.flowhrs * (sp.proccpu + sp.procgpu) / 2.0 / 
        (sp.remaingb * 10 * LOG(sm.facetverts + 10000)) as pru
    FROM scanprocessing sp
    LEFT JOIN scanmesh sm ON sp.zoneref = sm.zoneref
),
workflow_analysis AS (
    -- Step 2: Analyze workflow efficiency
    SELECT 
        rm.equipref,
        rm.pru,
        ROUND(AVG(rm.flowhrs) OVER (PARTITION BY rm.equipref), 2) as avg_processing_hours,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY rm.pru) as efficiency_rank,
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'cpu_usage', rm.proccpu,
            'gpu_usage', rm.procgpu,
            'processing_hours', rm.flowhrs
        ) as resource_details
    FROM resource_metrics rm
)
SELECT 
    equipref,
    ROUND(pru::numeric, 2) as processing_resource_utilization,
    avg_processing_hours,
    efficiency_rank,
    CASE 
        WHEN pru < 5.0 THEN 'Optimized'
        WHEN pru < 10.0 THEN 'Acceptable'
        ELSE 'Needs Optimization'
    END as workflow_status,
    resource_details
FROM workflow_analysis
ORDER BY pru ASC;

/* 
 * SQL Intent: Create materialized view for high fidelity meshes
 * Knowledge: #4 (Mesh Complexity Ratio), #13 (High Fidelity Mesh)
 * Advanced Features: Materialized view, Function index
 */

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW high_fidelity_meshes AS
SELECT
    sm.facetregistry,
    sm.zoneref,
    sm.equipref,
    sm.facetverts,
    sm.facetfaces,
    sm.facetresmm,
    sm.geomdeltamm,
    -- Calculate MCR per knowledge #4
    (sm.facetfaces::float / NULLIF(sm.facetverts, 0) / POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2)) * 1000 AS mcr
FROM scanmesh sm
WHERE 
    -- Apply High Fidelity Mesh criteria from knowledge #13
    (sm.facetfaces::float / NULLIF(sm.facetverts, 0) / POWER(sm.facetresmm, 2)) * 1000 > 5.0 AND
    sm.facetresmm < 1.0 AND
    sm.geomdeltamm < 0.5;

sol_sql:
-- Intent: Define a reusable function to calculate the SRI metric for scan quality assessment.
-- KB Used: 0 (Scan Resolution Index - SRI)
-- Advanced Features: CREATE FUNCTION, Mathematical functions (LOG), Parameter handling

-- Step 1: Define the function signature, specifying input parameters (resolution, density) and return type.
-- Step 2: Implement the SRI formula from KB 0 using PostgreSQL's mathematical functions. Handle potential division by zero or invalid log input.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_sri(scan_resolution_mm NUMERIC, point_density INTEGER)
RETURNS NUMERIC AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Step 2a: Check for invalid inputs to avoid errors (e.g., non-positive values for log).
    IF scan_resolution_mm <= 0 OR point_density <= 0 THEN
        RETURN NULL; -- Or raise an error, or return a specific value indicating invalid input
    END IF;
    -- Step 2b: Calculate SRI using the formula. Use NULLIF to prevent division by zero if log(point_density) is zero.
    RETURN (LOG(10, scan_resolution_mm * 1000) / NULLIF(LOG(10, point_density), 0)) * 5;
EXCEPTION
    -- Step 2c: Handle potential math errors (e.g., log of non-positive number if initial check missed something).
    WHEN numeric_value_out_of_range OR division_by_zero THEN
        RETURN NULL; -- Return NULL if calculation fails
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE; -- Mark as IMMUTABLE if output depends only on inputs
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):

    """
    ]

    return 1

-- Intent: Provide an easy way to query scans meeting the criteria for 'Premium Quality'.
-- KB Used: KB 10 (High Resolution), KB 11 (Comprehensive Coverage)
-- Advanced Features: CREATE VIEW, JOIN (LEFT JOIN), Multiple conditions

-- Step 1: Define the view structure.
-- Step 2: Join scans and scanpointcloud tables to access necessary metrics.
-- Step 3: Apply the criteria from KB 10 (Resolution, Density) and KB 11 (Coverage, Overlap).
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW view_premium_quality_scans AS
SELECT
    s.QuestRegistry,
    s.ArcRef,
    s.ZoneRef,
    s.ChronoTag,
    spc.ScanResolMm,
    spc.PointDense,
    spc.CoverPct,
    spc.LapPct,
    spc.NoiseDb
FROM
    public.scans s
LEFT JOIN -- Use LEFT JOIN in case some scans don't have point cloud data yet
    public.scanpointcloud spc ON s.ArcRef = spc.ArcRef AND s.CrewRef = spc.CrewRef -- Adjust join keys as needed
WHERE
    -- Step 3a: Apply High Resolution criteria (KB 10)
    spc.ScanResolMm <= 1.0 AND spc.PointDense >= 1000
    -- Step 3b: Apply Comprehensive Coverage criteria (KB 11)
    AND spc.CoverPct >= 95.0 AND spc.LapPct >= 30.0
    -- Step 3c: Add a basic noise check
    AND spc.NoiseDb < 1.5; -- Example threshold for low noise

    """

    POWER(ABS(humepct - 50) / 2, 1.5) -

sol_sql:
/* Intent: Delete redundant scans while keeping those with best quality (SQS) and ensuring comprehensive coverage
Knowledge used: 
- #3 (Scan Quality Score): SQS = (10/SRI)^1.5 × (SCE/100) × (1 - NoiseDb/30)^2
- #11 (Comprehensive Coverage): CoverPct ≥ 95 AND LapPct ≥ 30
Advanced features: DELETE with CTEs, Window functions, Multiple-step calculations */

DELETE FROM scans
USING (
    WITH scan_metrics AS (
        -- Step 1: Calculate SRI (Scan Resolution Index) 
        SELECT s.questregistry,
               s.arcref,
               sp.scanresolmm,
               sp.pointdense,
               sp.coverpct,
               sp.lappct,
               sp.noisedb,
               (LOG(10, sp.scanresolmm * 1000) / LOG(10, sp.pointdense) * 5) AS sri
        FROM scans s
        JOIN scanpointcloud sp USING (arcref)
        -- Filter 1: Only consider scans that don't meet comprehensive coverage standard
        WHERE sp.coverpct < 95 OR sp.lappct < 30
    ),
    quality_scores AS (
        -- Step 2: Calculate full SQS incorporating both SCE and noise factors
        SELECT questregistry,
               arcref,
               coverpct,
               lappct,
               -- Complete SQS calculation per Knowledge #3
               POWER(10/sri, 1.5) * 
               ((coverpct * (1 + lappct/100 * (1 - coverpct/100)))/100) *
               POWER(1 - noisedb/30, 2) AS sqs,
               -- Rank scans within each project based on quality
               ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                   PARTITION BY arcref 
                   ORDER BY 
                       -- First prioritize scans meeting coverage standards
                       CASE WHEN coverpct >= 95 AND lappct >= 30 THEN 1 
                            ELSE 2 END,
                       -- Then order by SQS for quality
                       POWER(10/sri, 1.5) * 
                       ((coverpct * (1 + lappct/100 * (1 - coverpct/100)))/100) *
                       POWER(1 - noisedb/30, 2) DESC
               ) AS quality_rank
        FROM scan_metrics
    )
    -- Step 3: Select redundant scans for deletion
    SELECT questregistry
    FROM quality_scores
    WHERE quality_rank > 1  -- Keep only the best scan per project
) redundant
WHERE scans.questregistry = redundant.questregistry
RETURNING scans.questregistry, scans.arcref;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # Identify a specific scan expected to be deleted based on the logic/data (using the example from original test)
    expected_deleted_questregistry = 'ASD648638'
    expected_deleted_arcref = 'PR8078'

    assert pred_query_result is not None, "DELETE query execution failed or did not return results via RETURNING."
    if not pred_query_result:
        print("Warning: DELETE statement did not return any rows. No redundant scans found or query logic error.")
        count_after_sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM scans;"
        count_after_result = execute_queries([count_after_sql], db_name, conn)
        final_scan_count = count_after_result[0][0][0] if count_after_result and count_after_result[0] else -1
        return 1 # Test passes if no rows were meant to be deleted

    returned_data = pred_query_result[0]
    num_deleted_returned = len(returned_data)
    assert num_deleted_returned > 0, "RETURNING clause should return at least one row."
    assert len(returned_data) == 2, f"RETURNING clause should return 2 columns (questregistry, arcref), but got {len(returned_data[0])}"

    # Check if the specific expected deletion was returned
    found_expected_deletion = any(row[0] == expected_deleted_questregistry and row[1] == expected_deleted_arcref for row in pred_query_result)
    assert found_expected_deletion,         f"Expected deleted scan ({expected_deleted_questregistry}, {expected_deleted_arcref}) not found in RETURNING output."

    # 2. Check table state AFTER deletion
    count_after_sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM scans;"
    count_after_result = execute_queries([count_after_sql], db_name, conn)
    final_scan_count = count_after_result[0][0][0] if count_after_result and count_after_result[0] else -1
    assert final_scan_count != -1, "Failed to get final scan count."

    # Verify the specific deleted scan is no longer in the table
    check_deleted_sql = f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM scans WHERE questregistry = '{expected_deleted_questregistry}';"
    check_deleted_result = execute_queries([check_deleted_sql], db_name, conn)
    assert check_deleted_result[0][0][0] == 0,         f"Scan {expected_deleted_questregistry} was expected to be deleted but still exists in the table."

    return 1

WITH HighRiskFlows AS (
    SELECT 
        df.RecordRegistry, 
        COALESCE(rm.RiskAssess / NULLIF(rm.CtrlEff, 0), 0) AS res, 
        COALESCE(dp.VolGB, 0) * 
            CASE 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'High' THEN 3 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'Medium' THEN 2 
                ELSE 1 
            END AS dsi
    FROM DataFlow df
    LEFT JOIN RiskManagement rm 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = rm.FlowLink
    LEFT JOIN DataProfile dp 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = dp.FlowSign
    WHERE COALESCE(rm.RiskAssess / NULLIF(rm.CtrlEff, 0), 0) > 0.7 
        AND COALESCE(dp.VolGB, 0) * 
            CASE 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'High' THEN 3 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'Medium' THEN 2 
                ELSE 1 
            END > 100
),
RankedFlows AS (
    SELECT 
        RecordRegistry, 
        res, 
        dsi, 
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY dsi DESC) AS volume_rank
    FROM HighRiskFlows
)
SELECT 
    RecordRegistry, 
    ROUND(res::numeric, 2) AS risk_exposure_score, 
    ROUND(dsi::numeric, 2) AS data_sensitivity_index
FROM RankedFlows
WHERE volume_rank <= 5
ORDER BY volume_rank
LIMIT 5;

WITH VendorRisk AS (
    SELECT 
        vm.VendorTrace, 
        CASE 
            WHEN vm.VendSecRate = 'A' THEN 4 
            WHEN vm.VendSecRate = 'B' THEN 3 
            WHEN vm.VendSecRate = 'C' THEN 2 
            ELSE 1 
        END * 
        CASE 
            WHEN vm.ContrState = 'Active' THEN 1 
            ELSE 0.5 
        END AS vri
    FROM VendorManagement vm
),
TieredVendors AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN vri < 2 THEN 'High Risk' 
            WHEN vri < 3 THEN 'Medium Risk' 
            ELSE 'Low Risk' 
        END AS risk_tier
    FROM VendorRisk
)
SELECT 
    risk_tier, 
    COUNT(*) AS vendor_count, 
    ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS percentage
FROM TieredVendors
GROUP BY risk_tier
ORDER BY vendor_count DESC LIMIT 100

WITH FlowMetrics AS (
    SELECT 
        df.RecordRegistry, 
        df.BwidthPct * df.DataSizeMB / df.DurMin AS bsi, 
        COALESCE(df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0), 0) AS dte
    FROM DataFlow df
    WHERE df.BwidthPct * df.DataSizeMB / df.DurMin > 50 
        AND COALESCE(df.SuccessPct / NULLIF(df.ErrTally + 1, 0), 0) < 1.0
)
SELECT 
    RecordRegistry, 
    ROUND(bsi::numeric, 2) AS bandwidth_saturation_index, 
    ROUND(dte::numeric, 2) AS data_transfer_efficiency
FROM FlowMetrics
ORDER BY bsi DESC
LIMIT 10

SELECT 
    JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
        'ProfileTrace', dp.ProfileTrace, 
        'DataSensitivityIndex', ROUND((dp.VolGB * 
            CASE 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'High' THEN 3 
                WHEN dp.DataSense = 'Medium' THEN 2 
                ELSE 1 
            END)::numeric, 2), 
        'SecurityRobustnessScore', 
            CASE 
                WHEN sp.EncState = 'Full' AND sp.AclState = 'Strong' THEN 3 
                WHEN sp.EncState = 'Full' OR sp.AclState = 'Strong' THEN 2 
                ELSE 1 
            END
    ) AS exposure_details
FROM DataProfile dp
LEFT JOIN SecurityProfile sp 
    ON dp.ProfileTrace = sp.ProfileKey
WHERE dp.VolGB * 
    CASE 
        WHEN dp.DataSense = 'High' THEN 3 
        WHEN dp.DataSense = 'Medium' THEN 2 
        ELSE 1 
    END > 100 
    AND CASE 
        WHEN sp.EncState = 'Full' AND sp.AclState = 'Strong' THEN 3 
        WHEN sp.EncState = 'Full' OR sp.AclState = 'Strong' THEN 2 
        ELSE 1 
    END < 2
ORDER BY dp.VolGB * 
    CASE 
        WHEN dp.DataSense = 'High' THEN 3 
        WHEN dp.DataSense = 'Medium' THEN 2 
        ELSE 1 
    END DESC
LIMIT 5

WITH NonCompliantFlows AS (
    SELECT 
        com.ComplianceTrace,
        com.GdprComp,
        com.LocLawComp,
        df.OrigNation,
        df.DestNation
    FROM Compliance com
    RIGHT JOIN DataFlow df 
        ON com.RecordRegistry = df.RecordRegistry
    WHERE EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM DataFlow df2 
        WHERE df2.RecordRegistry = df.RecordRegistry 
        AND df2.OrigNation != df2.DestNation
    )
    AND (
        com.GdprComp = 'Non-compliant' 
        OR com.LocLawComp = 'Non-compliant'
    )
)
SELECT 
    ComplianceTrace,
    GdprComp,
    LocLawComp,
    OrigNation || ' -> ' || DestNation AS transfer_path
FROM NonCompliantFlows
ORDER BY ComplianceTrace
LIMIT 10

WITH AuditSeverity AS (
    SELECT 
        ac.AuditTrace,
        COALESCE(ac.CritFindNum::float / NULLIF(ac.FindTally + 1, 0), 0) AS afs,
        ac.RemedDue
    FROM AuditAndCompliance ac
    WHERE ac.RemedDue IS NOT NULL
),
MonthlyTrends AS (
    SELECT 
        TO_CHAR(RemedDue, 'YYYY-MM') AS audit_month,
        AVG(afs) AS avg_severity,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(afs) DESC) AS severity_rank
    FROM AuditSeverity
    WHERE afs > 0.5
    GROUP BY TO_CHAR(RemedDue, 'YYYY-MM')
)
SELECT 
    audit_month,
    ROUND(avg_severity::numeric, 2) AS average_afs,
    severity_rank
FROM MonthlyTrends
WHERE severity_rank <= 3
ORDER BY audit_month

WITH RequestPressure AS (
    SELECT 
        ac.AuditTrace,
        (ac.AccReqNum + ac.DelReqNum + ac.RectReqNum + ac.PortReqNum) AS dsrl,
        (ac.AccReqNum + ac.DelReqNum + ac.RectReqNum + ac.PortReqNum) * ac.RespTimeDay AS dsrp,
        ARRAY[
            ('Access: ' || ac.AccReqNum)::text,
            ('Deletion: ' || ac.DelReqNum)::text,
            ('Rectification: ' || ac.RectReqNum)::text,
            ('Portability: ' || ac.PortReqNum)::text
        ] AS request_types
    FROM AuditAndCompliance ac
    WHERE ac.RespTimeDay IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    AuditTrace,
    ROUND(dsrp::numeric, 2) AS request_pressure,
    UNNEST(request_types) AS request_breakdown
FROM RequestPressure
WHERE dsrp > 50
ORDER BY dsrp DESC
LIMIT 100

WITH RiskVolume AS (
    SELECT 
        df.RecordRegistry,
        (rm.RiskAssess / rm.CtrlEff) * CASE 
            WHEN df.OrigNation != df.DestNation THEN 2 
            ELSE 1 
        END AS cbrf,
        ((rm.RiskAssess / rm.CtrlEff) * CASE 
            WHEN df.OrigNation != df.DestNation THEN 2 
            ELSE 1 
        END) * dp.VolGB AS cdvr
    FROM DataFlow df
    INNER JOIN RiskManagement rm 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = rm.FlowLink
    INNER JOIN DataProfile dp 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = dp.FlowSign
    WHERE df.OrigNation != df.DestNation
)
SELECT 
    RecordRegistry,
    ROUND(cbrf::numeric, 2) AS cross_border_risk_factor,
    ROUND(cdvr::numeric, 2) AS cross_border_data_volume_risk,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY cdvr DESC) AS risk_rank
FROM RiskVolume
WHERE cdvr > 1000
ORDER BY cdvr DESC
LIMIT 5

WITH IntegrityIssues AS (
    SELECT
        dp.ProfileTrace,
        dp.IntCheck,
        dp.CsumVerify,
        CASE
            WHEN dp.IntCheck = 'Failed' THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END + CASE
            WHEN dp.CsumVerify = 'Failed' THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS ifc
    FROM DataProfile dp
    WHERE dp.IntCheck = 'Failed'
        OR dp.CsumVerify = 'Failed'
)
SELECT
    ProfileTrace,
    ifc AS integrity_failure_count,
    TRIM(BOTH ', ' FROM CONCAT_WS(', ',
      CASE WHEN IntCheck   = 'Failed' THEN 'Integrity Check'        END,
      CASE WHEN CsumVerify = 'Failed' THEN 'Checksum Verification' END
    )) as failures
FROM IntegrityIssues
ORDER BY ifc DESC

WITH AuditPressure AS (
    SELECT 
        df.RecordRegistry AS flow_id,
        ((ac.CritFindNum::float / (ac.FindTally + 1)) * 
         (ac.AccReqNum + ac.DelReqNum + ac.RectReqNum + ac.PortReqNum) * 
         (ac.CritFindNum::float / (ac.FindTally + 1))) AS acp,
        '2025-04-01' - ac.RemedDue AS days_overdue
    FROM DataFlow df
    JOIN RiskManagement rm 
        ON df.RecordRegistry = rm.FlowLink
    JOIN DataProfile dp 
        ON dp.FlowSign = df.RecordRegistry
    JOIN AuditAndCompliance ac 
        ON ac.ProfJoin = dp.ProfileTrace
    WHERE df.OrigNation != df.DestNation 
        AND ac.RemedDue IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ap.flow_id,
    ROUND(ap.acp::numeric, 2) AS audit_compliance_pressure,
    ap.days_overdue
FROM AuditPressure ap
WHERE ap.acp > 5 
    AND ap.days_overdue < -5 
ORDER BY ap.days_overdue DESC, ap.acp DESC
LIMIT 10

WITH SensitiveDataProfiles AS (
    SELECT
        dp.profiletrace,
        COALESCE(dp.volgb, 0) *
        CASE dp.datasense
            WHEN 'High' THEN 3
            WHEN 'Medium' THEN 2
            WHEN 'Low' THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS dsi
    FROM dataprofile dp
)
UPDATE securityprofile sp
SET apisecstate = 'Review Required'
FROM SensitiveDataProfiles sdp
WHERE
    sp.profilekey = sdp.profiletrace
    AND sdp.dsi > 100
    AND (
        CASE
            WHEN sp.encstate::text = 'Full' AND sp.aclstate = 'Strong' THEN 3
            WHEN sp.encstate::text = 'Full' OR sp.aclstate = 'Adequate' THEN 2
            ELSE 1
        END
    ) < 2
    AND sp.apisecstate <> 'Review Required'
RETURNING *;

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS data_flow_metrics (
    recordregistry CHARACTER(8) PRIMARY KEY,
    last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    dte NUMERIC,
    success_pct NUMERIC,
    error_tally SMALLINT
);

-- Step 1: Define a CTE to calculate DTE for all data flows.
WITH FlowEfficiency AS (
    SELECT
        recordregistry,
        successpct,
        errtally,
        -- Calculate DTE based on KB definition (ID 0), ensuring no division by zero.
        CASE
            WHEN (errtally + 1) = 0 THEN NULL -- Or 0, depending on desired handling
            ELSE successpct / (errtally + 1.0) -- Use 1.0 for float division
        END AS calculated_dte
    FROM dataflow
)
-- Step 2: Insert new records or update existing ones in the metrics table.
INSERT INTO data_flow_metrics (recordregistry, dte, success_pct, error_tally, last_updated)
-- Step 3: Select the calculated data from the CTE.
SELECT
    fe.recordregistry,
    fe.calculated_dte,
    fe.successpct,
    fe.errtally,
    NOW() -- Update timestamp
FROM FlowEfficiency fe
-- Step 4: Specify the conflict target (the primary key).
ON CONFLICT (recordregistry)
-- Step 5: Define the update action if a conflict occurs (record exists).
DO UPDATE SET
    dte = EXCLUDED.dte,
    success_pct = EXCLUDED.success_pct,
    error_tally = EXCLUDED.error_tally,
    last_updated = EXCLUDED.last_updated;

-- Example: If 'CB932064' already exists in data_flow_metrics, its dte, success_pct, error_tally, and last_updated fields will be updated with the latest calculation. If it doesn't exist, a new row is inserted.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
        def is_close_enough(a, b, rel_tol=1e-9, abs_tol=1e-9):
            return abs(float(a) - float(b)) <= max(rel_tol * max(abs(float(a)), abs(float(b))), abs_tol)
        sample_query = """
            SELECT recordregistry, 
                successpct::numeric, 
                errtally::numeric 
            FROM dataflow 
            ORDER BY recordregistry 
            LIMIT 1;
        """
        res, _, _ = execute_queries([sample_query], db_name, conn)
        record_registry, success_pct, error_tally = res[0]

        expected_dte = round(success_pct / (error_tally + 1), 2)

        verification_query = f"""
            SELECT recordregistry, 
                ROUND(dte::numeric, 2) as dte, 
                success_pct::numeric, 
                error_tally::numeric
            FROM data_flow_metrics
            WHERE recordregistry = '{record_registry}';
        """
        res, _, _ = execute_queries([verification_query], db_name, conn)

        assert len(res) > 0, "No record was inserted into data_flow_metrics"
        assert res[0][1] == expected_dte, f"DTE value mismatch. Expected {expected_dte}, got {res[0][1]}"
        assert res[0][2] == success_pct, f"Success percentage mismatch. Expected {success_pct}, got {res[0][2]}"
        assert res[0][3] == error_tally, f"Error tally mismatch. Expected {error_tally}, got {res[0][3]}"

        update_source = f"""
        UPDATE dataflow 
        SET successpct = 95.0, errtally = 2
        WHERE recordregistry = '{record_registry}';
        """
        execute_queries([update_source], db_name, conn)
        execute_queries(pred_sqls, db_name, conn)

        new_success_pct = 95.0
        new_error_tally = 2
        new_expected_dte = round(new_success_pct / (new_error_tally + 1), 2)

        res, _, _ = execute_queries([verification_query], db_name, conn)

        assert is_close_enough(res[0][1], new_expected_dte), f"Updated DTE mismatch. Expected {new_expected_dte}, got {res[0][1]}"
        assert is_close_enough(float(res[0][2]), new_success_pct), f"Updated success_pct mismatch. Expected {new_success_pct}, got {res[0][2]}"
        assert res[0][3] == new_error_tally, f"Updated error_tally mismatch. Expected {new_error_tally}, got {res[0][3]}"
        return True

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW cross_border_compliance_gap_view AS
-- Step 1: Select relevant identifying information for the data flow and compliance status.
SELECT
    df.recordregistry,
    df.flowtag,
    df.orignation,
    df.destnation,
    c.gdprcomp,
    c.loclawcomp,
    vm.vendortrace -- Include vendor info for context
-- Step 2: Start with the dataflow table as the base.
FROM dataflow df
-- Step 3: Join with compliance data via intermediate tables (risk -> compliance). Requires joining through riskmanagement first.
JOIN riskmanagement rm ON df.recordregistry = rm.flowlink
JOIN compliance c ON rm.risktrace = c.risktie
-- Step 4: Join with vendor management (optional, but useful context).
LEFT JOIN vendormanagement vm ON c.vendortie = vm.vendortrace -- Use LEFT JOIN if vendor link isn't mandatory
-- Step 5: Filter for cross-border flows (origin != destination) based on KB ID 75.
WHERE df.orignation <> df.destnation
-- Step 6: Filter for compliance gaps in GDPR or Local Laws based on KB ID 15.
  AND (c.gdprcomp::text = 'Non-compliant' OR c.loclawcomp::text = 'Non-compliant')

-- Intent: Alter the dataflow table to add a new column storing the 'Transfer Path',
-- then populate this column based on origin and destination nations for 'Cross-Border Data Flows'.

-- Step 1: Add the new column to the dataflow table.
ALTER TABLE dataflow
ADD COLUMN transfer_path VARCHAR(255);

-- Add comment to the new column
COMMENT ON COLUMN dataflow.transfer_path IS 'Stores the transfer path (OrigNation -> DestNation) as defined in KB ID 70.';

-- Step 2: Update the new column for existing rows.
UPDATE dataflow
-- Step 3: Concatenate OrigNation and DestNation using the ' -> ' separator.
SET transfer_path = orignation || ' -> ' || destnation
-- Step 4: Apply this update only to rows identified as 'Cross-Border Data Flow' (where origin != destination).
WHERE orignation IS NOT NULL
  AND destnation IS NOT NULL
  AND orignation <> destnation;

-- External Knowledge Used:
--   - KB ID 70: Transfer Path (Definition used for concatenation format)
--   - KB ID 75: Cross-Border Data Flow (Definition used for WHERE clause)
-- Advanced PSQL Features Used:
--   - ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN statement
--   - UPDATE statement
--   - String concatenation operator (||)
--   - COMMENT ON statement
test_cases:

        record_registry = res[0][0]

            WHERE recordregistry = '{record_registry}';

        res, _, _ = execute_queries([validation_query], db_name, conn)

        row = res[0]
        origin = row[0]

        actual_path = row[2]
        expected_path = f"{origin} -> {destination}"

        print(f"Checking data for {record_registry}:")
        print(f"  Origin: {origin}")
        print(f"  Destination: {destination}")
        print(f"  Actual Path: {actual_path}")
        print(f"  Expected Path: {expected_path}")

        # Check 1: Is it actually a cross-border flow?
        assert origin != destination, f"Test record {record_registry} is not cross-border ({origin} == {destination})"
        # Check 2: Is the transfer_path populated correctly?
        assert actual_path == expected_path, f"Transfer path mismatch for {record_registry}. Expected '{expected_path}', got '{actual_path}'"
        print("  Verified: Transfer Path format and content are correct.")

-- Intent: Update the remediation status in the auditandcompliance table to 'Overdue'
-- for audits identified as having a 'Critical Audit Issue' where the remediation due date has passed.
UPDATE auditandcompliance ac
-- Step 1: Set the remedstate column to 'Overdue'.
SET remedstate = 'Overdue'
-- Step 2: Calculate Audit Finding Severity (AFS) inline using data from the same table.
--          AFS = CritFindNum / (FindTally + 1). Add 1 to FindTally to prevent division by zero.
-- Step 3: Filter rows where AFS meets the 'Critical Audit Issue' threshold (> 0.5).
WHERE (ac.critfindnum::numeric / (ac.findtally + 1)) > 0.5
-- Step 4: Further filter rows where the remediation due date (remeddue) is in the past.
  AND ac.remeddue < CURRENT_DATE
-- Step 5: Also ensure we only update records that aren't already marked 'Complete' or 'Overdue'.
  AND ac.remedstate NOT IN ('Complete', 'Overdue');

-- External Knowledge Used:
--   - KB ID 7: Audit Finding Severity (AFS) (Calculation used in WHERE)
--   - KB ID 13: Critical Audit Issue (Definition used for filtering threshold)
-- Advanced PSQL Features Used:
--   - UPDATE statement
--   - CURRENT_DATE function
--   - Arithmetic operations within WHERE clause
--   - Type casting (::numeric)
--   - Filtering using NOT IN

-- Intent: Analyze the relationship between Equipment Efficiency Rating (EER) and safety index
-- This uses knowledge ID 0 and window functions to rank equipment

-- Step 1: Calculate EER for all equipment using the formula from KB
-- EER = (performanceindex + reliabilityindex) / 2 * (1 - environmentalimpactindex / 10)
WITH equipment_efficiency AS (
    SELECT 
        equipmentcode,
        equipmenttype,
        ((performanceindex + reliabilityindex) / 2 * (1 - environmentalimpactindex / 10))::numeric(10,2) AS eer,
        safetyindex
    FROM equipment
)
-- Step 2: Analyze correlation between EER and safety across equipment types
-- Using window functions to rank equipment within their type by EER
SELECT
    equipmenttype,
    equipmentcode,
    eer,
    safetyindex,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY equipmenttype ORDER BY eer DESC) AS efficiency_rank,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY equipmenttype ORDER BY safetyindex DESC) AS safety_rank,
    ABS(RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY equipmenttype ORDER BY eer DESC) - 
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY equipmenttype ORDER BY safetyindex DESC)) AS rank_difference
FROM equipment_efficiency
ORDER BY equipmenttype, eer DESC;

-- Intent: Identify equipment that meets Extreme Weather Readiness (EWR) criteria
-- Uses knowledge ID 10, ID 50 with JOIN operations and conditional logic

-- Step 1: Calculate SSF (Structural Safety Factor) from weatherandstructure table
WITH structural_safety AS (
    SELECT 
        ws.weatherlocref,
        ROUND((
            (100 - ws.structuralloadpercent) / 100 *
            CASE 
                WHEN ws.snowloadkgm2 > 100 OR ws.windspeedms > 20 THEN 0.5
                WHEN ws.snowloadkgm2 > 50 OR ws.windspeedms > 10 THEN 0.8
                ELSE 1.0
            END
        )::numeric, 2) AS ssf
    FROM weatherandstructure ws
)
-- Step 2: Identify equipment with proper EWR conditions
-- Joining several tables to check all conditions from knowledge ID 10
SELECT 
    e.equipmentcode,
    e.equipmenttype,
    ce.heaterstatus,
    tswg.insulationstatus,
    ls.emergencylightstatus,
    ss.ssf,
    CASE 
        WHEN ss.ssf > 0.7 
             AND ce.heaterstatus != 'Off'
             AND tswg.insulationstatus != 'Poor'
             AND (ls.emergencylightstatus = 'On' OR ls.emergencylightstatus = 'Testing')
        THEN 'Extreme Weather Ready'
        ELSE 'Not Ready'
    END AS ewr_status
FROM equipment e
JOIN location l ON e.equipmentcode = l.loceqref
LEFT JOIN cabinenvironment ce ON e.equipmentcode = ce.cabineqref
LEFT JOIN lightingandsafety ls ON e.equipmentcode = ls.lighteqref
LEFT JOIN thermalsolarwindandgrid tswg ON e.equipmentcode = tswg.thermaleqref
LEFT JOIN structural_safety ss ON l.locationregistry = ss.weatherlocref
WHERE ss.ssf IS NOT NULL;

-- Intent: Calculate Life Support System Reliability (LSSR) for critical equipment
-- Uses knowledge IDs 33, 1, 51 and 6 with advanced JSON processing

-- Step 1: Calculate ORS (Operational Readiness Score) for equipment
WITH ors_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        om.opmainteqref,
        CASE 
            WHEN om.operationalstatus = 'Active' THEN 10 * (1 - om.operationhours / om.maintenancecyclehours)
            WHEN om.operationalstatus = 'Standby' THEN 5 * (1 - om.operationhours / om.maintenancecyclehours)
            ELSE 0 
        END AS ors
    FROM operationmaintenance om
),
-- Step 2: Calculate TIE (Thermal Insulation Efficiency) for each structure
tie_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        thermaleqref,
        CASE 
            WHEN insulationstatus = 'Good' THEN 0.9 - (heatlossratekwh / 10)
            WHEN insulationstatus = 'Fair' THEN 0.6 - (heatlossratekwh / 10)
            WHEN insulationstatus = 'Poor' THEN 0.3 - (heatlossratekwh / 10)
            ELSE 0
        END AS tie
    FROM thermalsolarwindandgrid
),
-- Step 3: Calculate LSSR (Life Support System Reliability) using the formula
-- LSSR = 0.7 * ORS + 0.3 * TIE
lssr_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        oc.opmainteqref,
        0.7 * oc.ors + 0.3 * COALESCE(tc.tie, 0) AS lssr
    FROM ors_calc oc
    LEFT JOIN tie_calc tc ON oc.opmainteqref = tc.thermaleqref
)
-- Step 4: Analyze life support systems reliability
SELECT 
    e.equipmentcode,
    e.equipmenttype,
    ls.lifesupportstatus,
    ROUND(lc.lssr::numeric, 2) AS lssr,
    CASE 
        WHEN lc.lssr >= 0.8 THEN 'High Reliability'
        WHEN lc.lssr >= 0.6 THEN 'Moderate Reliability'
        ELSE 'Low Reliability' 
    END AS reliability_classification,
    jsonb_build_object(
        'oxygen_status', ls.oxygensupplystatus,
        'medical_status', ls.medicalequipmentstatus,
        'safety_system_status', ls.safetysystemstatus
    ) AS support_systems_status
FROM equipment e
JOIN lssr_calc lc ON e.equipmentcode = lc.opmainteqref
JOIN lightingandsafety ls ON e.equipmentcode = ls.lighteqref
WHERE e.equipmenttype = 'Safety'
ORDER BY lssr DESC;

-- Intent: Analyze Renewable Energy Contribution (REC) across different locations
-- Uses knowledge ID 9, ID 52 with JSON array functions and location-based aggregation

-- Step 1: Extract renewable energy metrics and calculate REC for each equipment
WITH renewable_contribution AS (
    SELECT 
        tswg.thermaleqref,
        l.stationname,
        l.locationtype,
        tswg.renewablemetrics->>'solar' AS solar_json,
        tswg.renewablemetrics->>'wind' AS wind_json,
        tswg.fuelcelloutputw,
        (tswg.renewablemetrics->'solar'->>'output_w')::numeric + 
        (tswg.renewablemetrics->'wind'->>'output_w')::numeric AS renewable_output,
        ((tswg.renewablemetrics->'solar'->>'output_w')::numeric + 
         (tswg.renewablemetrics->'wind'->>'output_w')::numeric) / 
        NULLIF((tswg.fuelcelloutputw + 
               (tswg.renewablemetrics->'solar'->>'output_w')::numeric + 
               (tswg.renewablemetrics->'wind'->>'output_w')::numeric), 0) * 100 AS rec
    FROM thermalsolarwindandgrid tswg
    JOIN equipment e ON tswg.thermaleqref = e.equipmentcode
    JOIN location l ON e.equipmentcode = l.loceqref
    WHERE (tswg.renewablemetrics->'solar'->>'output_w') IS NOT NULL 
      OR (tswg.renewablemetrics->'wind'->>'output_w') IS NOT NULL
)
-- Step 2: Analyze REC by location type and station
SELECT 
    locationtype,
    stationname,
    COUNT(*) AS equipment_count,
    ROUND(rec::numeric, 2) AS rec,
    ROUND(SUM(renewable_output)::numeric, 2) AS total_renewable_output_w,
    CASE 
        WHEN rec > 70 THEN 'Energy-Sustainable'
        WHEN rec > 50 THEN 'Moderately Sustainable'
        ELSE 'Low Sustainability'
    END AS sustainability_classification
FROM renewable_contribution
GROUP BY locationtype, stationname, rec
ORDER BY rec DESC;

-- Intent: Analyze water resource management efficiency and its relationship to water quality
-- Uses knowledge IDs 7, 18, 25, 53 with conditional aggregation

-- Step 1: Calculate WRMI (Water Resource Management Index) for each water system
WITH wrmi_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        waw.watereqref,
        waw.waterlevelpercent * 
        (waw.waterqualityindex / 100) * 
        (1 - waw.wastetanklevelpercent / 100) AS wrmi
    FROM waterandwaste waw
)
-- Step 2: Analyze water resource management by station and water quality level
SELECT 
    l.stationname,
    l.locationtype,
    ROUND(AVG(waw.waterqualityindex)::numeric, 2) AS avg_water_quality_index,
    ROUND(AVG(wc.wrmi)::numeric, 2) AS avg_wrmi,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE wc.wrmi < 0.5) AS systems_needing_conservation,
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(waw.waterqualityindex) >= 91 THEN 'High-Quality'
        WHEN AVG(waw.waterqualityindex) >= 71 THEN 'Good'
        WHEN AVG(waw.waterqualityindex) >= 51 THEN 'Moderate'
        WHEN AVG(waw.waterqualityindex) >= 26 THEN 'Poor'
        ELSE 'Unsafe'
    END AS quality_classification,
    CASE 
        WHEN AVG(wc.wrmi) < 0.5 THEN 'Conservation Needed'
        WHEN AVG(wc.wrmi) < 0.7 THEN 'Monitoring Advised'
        ELSE 'Sustainable Management'
    END AS management_status
FROM waterandwaste waw
JOIN equipment e ON waw.watereqref = e.equipmentcode
JOIN location l ON e.equipmentcode = l.loceqref
JOIN wrmi_calc wc ON waw.watereqref = wc.watereqref
GROUP BY l.stationname, l.locationtype
ORDER BY avg_wrmi DESC, avg_water_quality_index DESC;

-- Intent: Calculate the Operational Readiness Score (ORS) for each operation/maintenance record.
-- External Knowledge used: "Operational Readiness Score (ORS)" (id:1)
-- Advanced SQL function: CASE expression.
SELECT 
    opmaintregistry, 
    operationhours,
    maintenancecyclehours,
    operationalstatus,
    -- Step 1: Use CASE to check operational status.
    -- Step 2: For 'Active', multiply by 10; for 'Standby', multiply by 5; otherwise 0.
    CASE 
        WHEN operationalstatus = 'Active' THEN 10 * (1 - operationhours/maintenancecyclehours)
        WHEN operationalstatus = 'Standby' THEN 5 * (1 - operationhours/maintenancecyclehours)
        ELSE 0 
    END AS ORS
FROM public.operationmaintenance;

-- Intent: Compute the Energy Sustainability Index (ESI) from power battery data.
-- External Knowledge used: "Energy Sustainability Index (ESI)" (id:2)
-- Advanced SQL function: CASE expression.
SELECT 
    pwrbatteqref, 
    energyefficiencypercent, 
    powersource,
    -- Step 1: Use CASE to adjust efficiency based on the power source.
    CASE 
        WHEN powersource IN ('Solar', 'Wind') THEN energyefficiencypercent * 1.5
        WHEN powersource = 'Hybrid' THEN energyefficiencypercent * 1.2
        WHEN powersource = 'Battery' THEN energyefficiencypercent * 1.0
        WHEN powersource = 'Diesel' THEN energyefficiencypercent * 0.7
        ELSE 0 
    END AS ESI
FROM public.powerbattery;

-- Intent: Calculate Base Station Communication Stability Index (BSCSI) by combining CRI and radio strength metrics.
-- External Knowledge used: "Base Station Communication Stability Index (BSCSI)" (id:32), id: 4
-- Advanced SQL functions: JSON extraction, complex nested CASE expressions.
SELECT 
    commregistry, 
    antennastatus,
    -- Step 1: Extract radio_strength_dbm and latency_ms from JSON in 'signalmetrics'.
    ROUND((jsonb_extract_path_text(signalmetrics, 'radio_strength_dbm'))::numeric, 2) AS radio_strength,
    ROUND((jsonb_extract_path_text(signalmetrics, 'latency_ms'))::numeric, 2) AS latency_ms,
    -- Step 2: Calculate CRI based on antennastatus.
    ROUND(
        CASE 
            WHEN antennastatus = 'Error' THEN 0 
            WHEN antennastatus = 'Warning' THEN 5 
            WHEN antennastatus = 'Normal' THEN 10 
            ELSE 0 
        END * (1 - (jsonb_extract_path_text(signalmetrics, 'latency_ms'))::numeric/1000), 
        2
    ) AS CRI,
    -- Step 3: Compute BSCSI using CRI and adjustments from radio strength and latency.
    ROUND(
        CASE 
            WHEN (jsonb_extract_path_text(signalmetrics, 'latency_ms'))::numeric IS NOT NULL THEN
                (CASE 
                    WHEN antennastatus = 'Error' THEN 0 
                    WHEN antennastatus = 'Warning' THEN 5 
                    WHEN antennastatus = 'Normal' THEN 10 
                    ELSE 0 
                END)
                * (1 + 0.2 * ((jsonb_extract_path_text(signalmetrics, 'radio_strength_dbm'))::numeric/100))
                * (1 - 0.01 * (1000 - (jsonb_extract_path_text(signalmetrics, 'latency_ms'))::numeric))
            ELSE NULL 
        END, 
        2
    ) AS BSCSI
FROM public.communication;

-- Intent: Identify equipment with high Overall Safety Performance Index (OSPI) based on safetyindex and computed EER.
-- External Knowledge used: "Overall Safety Performance Index (OSPI)" (id:30), id: 0
-- Advanced SQL functions: Computed columns with arithmetic operations and filtering in WHERE clause.
SELECT 
    equipmentcode,
    -- Compute EER
    ((performanceindex + reliabilityindex)/2) * (1 - environmentalimpactindex/10) AS EER,
    -- Compute OSPI
    safetyindex * (((performanceindex + reliabilityindex)/2) * (1 - environmentalimpactindex/10)) * 0.8 AS OSPI
FROM public.equipment
-- Filter records with OSPI > 0.75
WHERE safetyindex * (((performanceindex + reliabilityindex)/2) * (1 - environmentalimpactindex/10)) * 0.8 > 0.75;

-- Intent: Compute Vehicle Performance Coefficient (VPC) joining chassis and engine data.
-- External Knowledge used: "Vehicle Performance Coefficient (VPC)" (id:5)
-- Advanced SQL functions: LEFT JOIN, arithmetic calculations.
SELECT 
    cv.chassisregistry, 
    -- Calculate VPC using the given formula
    (1 - ((cv.brakepadwearpercent + cv.trackwearpercent)/200.0)) * (cv.vehiclespeedkmh/50.0) * (ef.engineloadpercent/100.0) AS VPC
FROM public.chassisandvehicle cv
LEFT JOIN public.engineandfluids ef ON cv.chassisengref = ef.engineregistry;

-- Intent: Create a function to automatically calculate ESI (Energy Sustainability Index)
-- Knowledge used: #2 Energy Sustainability Index (ESI)
-- Advanced features: CASE expression, function creation

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_esi(
    efficiency NUMERIC, 
    source VARCHAR
) RETURNS NUMERIC AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Step 1: Calculate ESI value based on energy sustainability formula
    -- Using CASE expression to implement different weights for energy source types
    RETURN efficiency * CASE 
        WHEN source = 'Solar' THEN 1.5
        WHEN source = 'Wind' THEN 1.5
        WHEN source = 'Hybrid' THEN 1.2
        WHEN source = 'Battery' THEN 1.0
        WHEN source = 'Diesel' THEN 0.7
        ELSE 0
    END;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Intent: Update equipment that meets cabin habitability standards
-- Knowledge used: #17 Cabin Habitability Standard
-- Advanced features: JOIN with subquery and JSON access

UPDATE equipment e
SET reliabilityindex = reliabilityindex * 1.15
WHERE equipmentcode IN (
    -- Step 1: Identify equipment that meets cabin habitability standards
    SELECT c.cabineqref FROM cabinenvironment c
    JOIN thermalsolarwindandgrid t ON c.cabineqref = t.thermaleqref
    JOIN weatherandstructure w ON c.cabinlocref = w.weatherlocref
    WHERE (c.cabinclimate->>'temperature_c')::real BETWEEN 18 AND 24
    AND (c.cabinclimate->>'o2_percent')::numeric > 19.5
    AND (c.cabinclimate->>'co2_ppm')::numeric < 1000
    AND c.ventilationstatus != 'Off'
    AND c.heaterstatus != 'Off'
);

-- Intent: Provide an easily queryable view for water resource status
-- Knowledge used: #7 Water Resource Management Index (WRMI), #53
-- Advanced features: View creation with calculation

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW water_management_view AS
SELECT 
    w.watereqref,
    -- Step 1: Calculate WRMI according to formula
    w.waterlevelpercent * (w.waterqualityindex/100.0) * (1 - w.wastetanklevelpercent/100.0) AS wrmi,
    -- Step 2: Classify water management status
    CASE
        WHEN w.waterlevelpercent * (w.waterqualityindex/100.0) * (1 - w.wastetanklevelpercent/100.0) < 0.5 
            THEN 'Conservation Needed'
        WHEN w.waterlevelpercent * (w.waterqualityindex/100.0) * (1 - w.wastetanklevelpercent/100.0) < 0.7 
            THEN 'Monitoring Advised'
        ELSE 'Sustainable Management'
    END AS management_status
FROM waterandwaste w
WHERE w.waterlevelpercent IS NOT NULL;
