WITH SensorMetrics AS (
    SELECT 
        c.ContainRegistry,
        s.ContainLink,
        /* KB #0: Calculate TSS = (1 - TempDevCount/100) * (1 - CritEvents/10) * TempStabIdx */
        (1 - COALESCE(s.TempDevCount::FLOAT / 100, 0)) * 
        (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) * 
        COALESCE(s.TempStabIdx, 1) AS TSS,
        /* KB #9: Calculate TBS = |TempNowC - StoreTempC| / TempTolC * TempDevCount */
        ABS(s.TempNowC - s.StoreTempC) / NULLIF(s.TempTolC, 0) * COALESCE(s.TempDevCount, 0) AS TBS,
        s.AlertTime,
        /* KB #2: Calculate CRI = (1 - TSS) * (1 - CoolRemainPct/100) */
        (1 - ((1 - COALESCE(s.TempDevCount::FLOAT / 100, 0)) * 
              (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) * 
              COALESCE(s.TempStabIdx, 1))) * 
        (1 - COALESCE(c.CoolRemainPct, 100) / 100) AS CRI
    FROM Container c
    /* KB #10: LEFT JOIN ensures all containers are included, even without SensorData, to classify them as Stable if no data exists in past 1 year */
    LEFT JOIN SensorData s ON c.ContainRegistry = s.ContainLink
        AND s.AlertTime >= (SELECT MAX(AlertTime) - INTERVAL '1 year' FROM SensorData)
        /* KB #10: Restrict to SensorData from the past 1 year relative to the latest AlertTime */
),
StabilityMetrics AS (
    SELECT 
        ContainRegistry,
        /* KB #10: Average TSS over past 1 year for stability trends, default to 1.0 if no data */
        COALESCE(AVG(TSS), 1.0) AS avg_TSS,
        /* KB #10: Maximum TBS over past 1 year for breach severity, default to 0.0 if no data */
        COALESCE(MAX(TBS), 0.0) AS max_TBS,
        /* KB #10: Latest TSS within past 1 year for Critical status check, default to 1.0 if no data */
        COALESCE(MAX(CASE WHEN AlertTime = (SELECT MAX(AlertTime) FROM SensorMetrics sm2 
                          WHERE sm2.ContainRegistry = sm.ContainRegistry) 
                     THEN TSS END), 1.0) AS current_TSS,
        /* KB #10: Latest CRI within past 1 year for Critical status check, default to 0.0 if no data */
        COALESCE(MAX(CASE WHEN AlertTime = (SELECT MAX(AlertTime) FROM SensorMetrics sm2 
                          WHERE sm2.ContainRegistry = sm.ContainRegistry) 
                     THEN CRI END), 0.0) AS CRI
    FROM SensorMetrics sm
    GROUP BY ContainRegistry
)
SELECT 
    ContainRegistry AS ContainLink,
    ROUND(avg_TSS::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_TSS,
    ROUND(max_TBS::NUMERIC, 2) AS max_TBS,
    /* KB #10: Apply Container Health Status classification */
    CASE 
        WHEN CRI > 0.6 OR current_TSS < 0.4 THEN 'Critical' /* KB #10: Critical if CRI > 0.6 or current TSS < 0.4 */
        WHEN avg_TSS < 0.4 OR max_TBS > 1.5 THEN 'Unstable' /* KB #10: Unstable if avg_TSS < 0.4 or max_TBS > 1.5, not Critical */
        WHEN avg_TSS >= 0.7 AND max_TBS <= 1.0 THEN 'Stable' /* KB #10: Stable if avg_TSS >= 0.7 and max_TBS <= 1.0, not Critical */
        ELSE 'Moderate' /* KB #10: Moderate for remaining cases, not Critical */
    END AS container_health_status
FROM StabilityMetrics
LIMIT 100 /* Limit to 100 containers for manageable output */

WITH StabilityMetrics AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate TSS and TBS per KB #0 and KB #9, joining with LEFT JOIN
    -- By using LEFT JOIN to include containers without sensor data, ensuring all containers are evaluated
    SELECT 
        c.ContainRegistry,
        (1 - COALESCE(s.TempDevCount::FLOAT / 100, 0)) * 
        (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) * 
        COALESCE(s.TempStabIdx, 1) AS TSS,
        COALESCE(
            ABS(s.TempNowC - s.StoreTempC) / NULLIF(s.TempTolC, 0) * s.TempDevCount,
            0
        ) AS TBS,
        s.TempDevCount,
        s.CritEvents,
        s.TempStabIdx,
        s.TempNowC,
        s.StoreTempC,
        s.TempTolC,
        s.AlertTime,
        -- Diagnostic: Flag sensor data availability
        CASE 
            WHEN s.SensorTrack IS NULL THEN 'No Sensor Data'
            ELSE 'Sensor Data Available'
        END AS SensorDataStatus
    FROM Container c
    LEFT JOIN SensorData s ON c.ContainRegistry = s.ContainLink
    WHERE s.AlertTime IS NULL OR s.AlertTime >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
),
RiskIndex AS (
    -- Step 2: Calculate CRI and aggregates per KB #2, including diagnostic JSON
    -- By aggregating TSS and TBS over the past year for Container Health Status (KB #10)
    SELECT 
        sm.ContainRegistry,
        COALESCE(MAX(sm.TSS), 1.0) AS TSS,  -- Aggregate TSS to handle multiple sensor records
        COALESCE(MAX(sm.TBS), 0.0) AS max_TBS,
        (1 - COALESCE(MAX(sm.TSS), 1.0)) * (1 - COALESCE(c.CoolRemainPct, 0) / 100) AS CRI,
        MAX(sm.SensorDataStatus) AS SensorDataStatus,  -- Take latest status
        -- Step 3: Rank containers by CRI per KB #2
        -- By using RANK to prioritize high-risk containers
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY (1 - COALESCE(MAX(sm.TSS), 1.0)) * (1 - COALESCE(c.CoolRemainPct, 0) / 100) DESC) AS RiskRank,
        -- Diagnostic: Build JSON with aggregated metrics
        json_build_object(
            'TempDevCount', MAX(sm.TempDevCount),  -- Aggregate to avoid GROUP BY error
            'CritEvents', MAX(sm.CritEvents),      -- Aggregate to avoid GROUP BY error
            'TempStabIdx', MAX(sm.TempStabIdx),   -- Aggregate to avoid GROUP BY error
            'CoolRemainPct', c.CoolRemainPct,
            'TempBreachSeverity', MAX(sm.TBS)      -- Aggregate to avoid GROUP BY error
        ) AS DiagnosticInfo
    FROM StabilityMetrics sm
    LEFT JOIN Container c ON sm.ContainRegistry = c.ContainRegistry
    GROUP BY sm.ContainRegistry, c.CoolRemainPct
)
-- Step 4: Apply Critical criteria from Container Health Status (KB #10)
-- By filtering for CRI > 0.6 OR TSS < 0.4 to identify Critical containers
SELECT 
    ContainRegistry,
    ROUND(TSS::NUMERIC, 3) AS TSS,
    ROUND(CRI::NUMERIC, 3) AS CRI,
    RiskRank,
    SensorDataStatus,
    DiagnosticInfo
FROM RiskIndex
WHERE CRI > 0.6 OR TSS < 0.4
ORDER BY RiskRank
LIMIT 10

WITH RouteProgress AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate RCP for each transport
    -- RCP = (DistDoneKm / (DistDoneKm + DistLeftKm)) * 100
    SELECT 
        t.VehicleReg,
        t.ShipTransit,
        t.DistDoneKm,
        t.DistLeftKm,
        (t.DistDoneKm / NULLIF(t.DistDoneKm + t.DistLeftKm, 0)) * 100 AS RCP
    FROM TransportInfo t
    WHERE t.DistDoneKm IS NOT NULL AND t.DistLeftKm IS NOT NULL
),
ContainerRisk AS (
    -- Step 2: Calculate CRI for containers linked to shipments
    -- CRI = (1 - TSS) * (1 - CoolRemainPct/100)
    SELECT 
        c.ContainRegistry,
        c.ShipOwn,
        c.CoolRemainPct,
        (1 - ((1 - COALESCE(s.TempDevCount::FLOAT / 100, 0)) * 
              (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) * 
              COALESCE(s.TempStabIdx, 1))) * -- TSS
        (1 - COALESCE(c.CoolRemainPct, 0) / 100) AS CRI,
        s.TempDevCount,
        s.CritEvents,
        s.TempStabIdx
    FROM Container c
    FULL JOIN SensorData s ON c.ContainRegistry = s.ContainLink
    WHERE c.ShipOwn IS NOT NULL OR s.ContainLink IS NOT NULL
)
-- Step 3: Aggregate high-risk routes with direct fields
SELECT 
    rp.VehicleReg AS vehicle_id,
    rp.ShipTransit AS shipment_id,
    ROUND(rp.RCP::NUMERIC, 3) AS rcp,
    COALESCE(ROUND(MAX(cr.CRI)::NUMERIC, 3), 0) AS max_cri,
    rp.DistDoneKm AS dist_done_km,
    rp.DistLeftKm AS dist_left_km,
    MAX(cr.TempDevCount)::TEXT AS temp_dev_count,
    MAX(cr.CritEvents)::TEXT AS crit_events,
    MAX(cr.TempStabIdx)::TEXT AS temp_stab_idx,
    MAX(cr.CoolRemainPct)::TEXT AS cool_remain_pct
FROM RouteProgress rp
LEFT JOIN ContainerRisk cr ON rp.ShipTransit = cr.ShipOwn
WHERE rp.RCP < 50
GROUP BY rp.VehicleReg, rp.ShipTransit, rp.RCP, rp.DistDoneKm, rp.DistLeftKm
HAVING MAX(cr.CRI) > 0.4 OR MAX(cr.CRI) IS NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(MAX(cr.CRI), 0) DESC
LIMIT 10

WITH ShipmentRisk AS (
    -- Step 1: Flag compromised shipments per KB #11.
    -- Compromised: IntegrityMark='Compromised' OR SealFlag='Broken' OR TamperSign='Confirmed'
    SELECT
        s.ShipmentRegistry,
        CASE
            WHEN s.IntegrityMark = 'Compromised' OR s.SealFlag = 'Broken' OR s.TamperSign = 'Confirmed'
            THEN 'Compromised'
            ELSE 'Safe'
        END AS risk_status,
        COALESCE(rm.Incidents, 0) AS incident_count
    FROM Shipments s
    FULL JOIN RegulatoryAndMaintenance rm ON s.ShipmentRegistry = rm.ShipGov  -- Step 2: FULL JOIN for all shipments and maintenance records
)
-- Step 3: Aggregate incidents and rank shipments.
SELECT
    ShipmentRegistry,
    risk_status,
    incident_count,
    ARRAY_AGG(rm.RegMaintRegistry) AS maintenance_ids
FROM ShipmentRisk sr
LEFT JOIN RegulatoryAndMaintenance rm ON sr.ShipmentRegistry = rm.ShipGov
WHERE risk_status = 'Compromised'
GROUP BY ShipmentRegistry, risk_status, incident_count
ORDER BY incident_count DESC
LIMIT 50

WITH TransportStability AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate HQI and TSS for the most recent sensor reading per transport
    -- By using CROSS JOIN LATERAL to get the latest sensor data
    SELECT 
        t.VehicleReg,
        t.ShipTransit,
        (1 - COALESCE(s.HandleEvents::FLOAT / 100, 0)) * 
        (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) AS HQI,
        (1 - COALESCE(s.TempDevCount::FLOAT / 100, 0)) * 
        (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) * 
        COALESCE(s.TempStabIdx, 1) AS TSS
    FROM TransportInfo t
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
        SELECT * 
        FROM SensorData s 
        WHERE s.VehSenseRef = t.VehicleReg
        ORDER BY s.AlertTime DESC
        LIMIT 1
    ) s
)
-- Step 2: Filter stable transports per KB #14
-- By using WHERE to check HQI > 0.9 and TSS > 0.8
SELECT 
    VehicleReg,
    ShipTransit,
    ROUND(HQI::NUMERIC, 3) AS HQI,
    ROUND(TSS::NUMERIC, 3) AS TSS
FROM TransportStability
WHERE HQI > 0.9 AND TSS > 0.8
ORDER BY TSS DESC

SELECT 
    c.ContainRegistry,
    -- Step 1: Calculate TBS per sensor reading
    -- By using formula TBS = |TempNowC - StoreTempC| / TempTolC * TempDevCount
    ABS(s.TempNowC - s.StoreTempC) / NULLIF(s.TempTolC, 0) * s.TempDevCount AS TBS,
    s.TempDevCount,
    -- Step 2: Aggregate alert types into an array
    -- By using ARRAY_AGG with FILTER to collect non-null alerts
    ARRAY_AGG(s.AlertKind) FILTER (WHERE s.AlertKind IS NOT NULL) AS AlertTypes
FROM Container c
INNER JOIN SensorData s ON c.ContainRegistry = s.ContainLink
WHERE 
    -- Step 3: Apply KB #16 criteria
    -- By using WHERE to filter severe breaches
    ABS(s.TempNowC - s.StoreTempC) / NULLIF(s.TempTolC, 0) * s.TempDevCount > 2.0
    AND s.TempDevCount > 5
GROUP BY c.ContainRegistry, s.TempNowC, s.StoreTempC, s.TempTolC, s.TempDevCount
ORDER BY TBS DESC
LIMIT 5

WITH CoolantStatus AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate CDR for each container
    -- By using formula CDR = (100 - CoolRemainPct) / (Current_Date - RefillLatest)
    SELECT 
        c.ContainRegistry,
        c.CoolRemainPct,
        (100 - c.CoolRemainPct) / 
        NULLIF((CURRENT_DATE - c.RefillLatest)::INTEGER, 0) AS CDR,
        -- Step 2: Assign rank based on CDR
        -- By using DENSE_RANK to order by depletion rate
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY 
            (100 - c.CoolRemainPct) / NULLIF((CURRENT_DATE - c.RefillLatest)::INTEGER, 0) DESC
        ) AS DepletionRank
    FROM Container c
    WHERE c.RefillLatest IS NOT NULL
)
-- Step 3: Filter critical coolant conditions, adjusted for observed CDR range
-- By using WHERE to check CDR > 1.0 and CoolRemainPct < 30
SELECT 
    ContainRegistry,
    CoolRemainPct,
    ROUND(CDR::NUMERIC, 2) AS CDR,
    DepletionRank
FROM CoolantStatus
WHERE CDR > 1.0 AND CoolRemainPct < 30
ORDER BY DepletionRank
LIMIT 5

WITH EfficiencyMetrics AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate SER and TSS for containers
    -- By using FULL JOIN to include all containers, even without vaccine data
    SELECT 
        c.ContainRegistry,
        (v.VialTally * 10)::FLOAT / NULLIF(c.VolLiters, 0) AS SER,
        (1 - COALESCE(s.TempDevCount::FLOAT / 100, 0)) * 
        (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) * 
        COALESCE(s.TempStabIdx, 1) AS TSS,
        -- Step 2: Assign row numbers for top performers
        -- By using ROW_NUMBER to rank by SER
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY 
            (v.VialTally * 10)::FLOAT / NULLIF(c.VolLiters, 0) DESC NULLS LAST
        ) AS EfficiencyRank
    FROM Container c
    FULL JOIN VaccineDetails v ON c.ContainRegistry = v.ContainVac
    INNER JOIN SensorData s ON c.ContainRegistry = s.ContainLink
)
-- Step 3: Filter efficient containers per KB #18
-- By using WHERE to check SER > 0.8 and TSS > 0.9
SELECT 
    ContainRegistry,
    ROUND(SER::NUMERIC, 2) AS SER,
    ROUND(TSS::NUMERIC, 2) AS TSS,
    EfficiencyRank
FROM EfficiencyMetrics
WHERE SER > 0.8 AND TSS > 0.9
ORDER BY EfficiencyRank
LIMIT 5

WITH MaintenanceStatus AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate MCS per KB #7.
    -- MCS = CompScore * (1 - Incidents/10)
    SELECT
        rm.RegMaintRegistry,
        rm.CompScore,
        rm.Incidents,
        rm.MaintDateNext,
        rm.CalibDateNext,
        COALESCE(rm.CompScore * (1 - COALESCE(rm.Incidents, 0) / 10), 0) AS MCS
    FROM RegulatoryAndMaintenance rm
)
-- Step 2: Flag overdue equipment per KB #13.
SELECT
    RegMaintRegistry,
    ROUND(MCS::NUMERIC, 2) AS mcs,
    GREATEST(
        (CURRENT_DATE - MaintDateNext)::INTEGER,
        (CURRENT_DATE - CalibDateNext)::INTEGER,
        0
    ) AS days_overdue,
    'Overdue' AS maintenance_status
FROM MaintenanceStatus
WHERE MCS < 0.7 
  AND (CURRENT_DATE > MaintDateNext OR CURRENT_DATE > CalibDateNext)
  AND MCS IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY days_overdue DESC
LIMIT 5

WITH HandlingMetrics AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate HQI per KB #8.
    -- HQI = (1 - HandleEvents/100) * (1 - CritEvents/10)
    SELECT
        t.VehicleReg,
        t.TransMode,
        s.ContainLink,
        (1 - COALESCE(s.HandleEvents::FLOAT / 100, 0)) *
        (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) AS HQI,
        COALESCE(AVG(
            (1 - COALESCE(s.TempDevCount::FLOAT / 100, 0)) *
            (1 - COALESCE(s.CritEvents::FLOAT / 10, 0)) *
            COALESCE(s.TempStabIdx, 1)
        ), 1) AS TSS
    FROM TransportInfo t
    INNER JOIN SensorData s ON t.VehicleReg = s.VehSenseRef
    WHERE s.AlertTime >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'
    GROUP BY t.VehicleReg, t.TransMode, s.ContainLink, s.HandleEvents, s.CritEvents
)
-- Step 2: Aggregate and flag stable transports per KB #14.
SELECT
    TransMode,
    ROUND(AVG(HQI)::NUMERIC, 3) AS avg_hqi,
    ARRAY_TO_STRING(
        ARRAY_AGG(VehicleReg) FILTER (WHERE HQI > 0.9 AND TSS > 0.8),
        ','
    ) AS vehicles,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE HQI > 0.9 AND TSS > 0.8) AS stable_transports
FROM HandlingMetrics
GROUP BY TransMode
HAVING AVG(HQI) > 0
ORDER BY avg_hqi DESC
LIMIT 10;

-- Intent: Create a view that shows containers ranked by their coolant depletion rate (CDR),
-- identifying those potentially needing faster refills ('Depletion Rank').
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW container_depletion_rank_view AS
-- Step 1: Select container details and calculate CDR.
SELECT
    c.containregistry,
    c.containmodel,
    c.coolremainpct,
    c.refilllatest,
    -- Step 2: Calculate Coolant Depletion Rate (CDR) per day. Handle NULLs/edge cases.
    CASE
        WHEN c.refilllatest IS NULL OR (CURRENT_DATE - c.refilllatest) <= 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE (100.0 - c.coolremainpct) / (CURRENT_DATE - c.refilllatest)
    END AS coolant_depletion_rate_per_day,
    -- Step 3: Use the RANK() window function to assign rank based on descending CDR.
    --         Containers with higher depletion rates get lower rank numbers (e.g., Rank 1 is highest CDR).
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY
        CASE
            WHEN c.refilllatest IS NULL OR (CURRENT_DATE - c.refilllatest) <= 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE (100.0 - c.coolremainpct) / (CURRENT_DATE - c.refilllatest)
        END
    DESC NULLS LAST) AS depletion_rank
FROM
    container c;

-- Add comment to the view
COMMENT ON VIEW container_depletion_rank_view IS 'Ranks containers by coolant depletion rate (CDR - KB ID 1) based on Depletion Rank concept (KB ID 62). Higher CDR = Lower Rank number.';

-- External Knowledge Used:
--   - KB ID 62: Depletion Rank (Concept implemented using RANK())
--   - KB ID 1: Coolant Depletion Rate (CDR) (Calculation implemented)
-- Advanced PSQL Features Used:
--   - CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW statement
--   - Window Function (RANK() OVER(ORDER BY ... DESC NULLS LAST))
--   - CASE statement for safe calculation within SELECT and ORDER BY
--   - CURRENT_DATE function
--   - Date arithmetic
--   - COMMENT ON statement

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    from datetime import date

    sample_query = """
        SELECT
            containregistry,
            coolremainpct,
            refilllatest,
            coolant_depletion_rate_per_day,
            depletion_rank
        FROM container_depletion_rank_view
        ORDER BY depletion_rank ASC
        LIMIT 1;
    """
    res, _, _ = execute_queries([sample_query], db_name, conn)
    assert len(res) > 0, "No valid containers returned from view"

    containregistry, coolremainpct, refilllatest, db_cdr, rank = res[0]
    assert db_cdr is not None, f"CDR is NULL for container {containregistry}"
    assert rank == 1, f"Expected top rank to be 1, got {rank}"
    
    days_since_refill = (date.today() - refilllatest).days
    assert days_since_refill > 0, f"Refill date {refilllatest} is today or in future"
    expected_cdr = (100.0 - float(coolremainpct)) / days_since_refill

    # Step 5: Compare with DB result (allow tiny float diff)
    assert abs(expected_cdr - float(db_cdr)) < 0.01, f"CDR mismatch. Expected {expected_cdr}, got {db_cdr}"

    return True

-- Intent: Maintain a table ('container_tss_monitoring') with the latest calculated
-- Temperature Stability Score (TSS) for each container. Insert new entries or update existing ones.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS container_tss_monitoring (
   containregistry VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
   latest_tss REAL,
   last_updated TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE
);

-- Step 1: Use a CTE to calculate the current TSS for all containers based on latest sensor data.
--         This assumes one relevant sensor reading per container, potentially the latest one.
--         A more robust approach might use a window function to get the latest reading per container.
WITH current_tss AS (
    SELECT
        sd.containlink,
        -- Step 1a: Calculate TSS = (1 - TempDevCount/100) * (1 - CritEvents/10) * TempStabIdx.
        --          Use COALESCE to handle potential NULLs gracefully, defaulting to neutral values (0 deviations/events, 1.0 index).
        (1.0 - COALESCE(sd.tempdevcount, 0) / 100.0)
        * (1.0 - COALESCE(sd.critevents, 0) / 10.0)
        * COALESCE(sd.tempstabidx, 1.0) AS calculated_tss,
        NOW() as update_ts
        -- Step 1b: We need the *latest* sensor data per container. Use ROW_NUMBER()
        , ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY sd.containlink ORDER BY sd.alerttime DESC NULLS LAST, sd.sensortrack DESC) as rn
    FROM
        sensordata sd
)
-- Step 2: Use INSERT INTO ... ON CONFLICT (UPSERT).
INSERT INTO container_tss_monitoring (containregistry, latest_tss, last_updated)
SELECT
    containlink,
    calculated_tss,
    update_ts
FROM current_tss
-- Step 3: Only insert/update if TSS could be calculated and it's the latest record (rn=1).
WHERE calculated_tss IS NOT NULL AND rn = 1
-- Step 4: Specify the conflict target (the primary key).
ON CONFLICT (containregistry)
-- Step 5: Define the UPDATE action for conflicts.
DO UPDATE SET
    latest_tss = EXCLUDED.latest_tss,
    last_updated = EXCLUDED.last_updated;

-- External Knowledge Used:
--   - KB ID 0: Temperature Stability Score (TSS) (Calculation implemented in CTE)
-- Advanced PSQL Features Used:
--   - INSERT INTO ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE (UPSERT) statement
--   - Common Table Expression (CTE)
--   - Window Function (ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY ...))
--   - COALESCE function for NULL handling
--   - EXCLUDED pseudo-table in ON CONFLICT
--   - NOW() function

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
        def calculate_expected_tss(tempdevcount, critevents, tempstabidx):
            dev_count = tempdevcount if tempdevcount is not None else 0
            crit_events = critevents if critevents is not None else 0
            stab_idx = tempstabidx if tempstabidx is not None else 1.0

            tss = (1.0 - dev_count / 100.0) *                 (1.0 - crit_events / 10.0) *                 float(stab_idx) 
            return tss
        def is_close_enough(a, b, rel_tol=1e-9, abs_tol=1e-9):
            return abs(float(a) - float(b)) <= max(rel_tol * max(abs(float(a)), abs(float(b))), abs_tol)
        
        sample_query = """
            WITH LatestSensor AS (
            SELECT
                sd.containlink,
                sd.tempdevcount,
                sd.critevents,
                sd.tempstabidx,
                sd.sensortrack, -- Keep track for inserting a newer one
                ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY sd.containlink ORDER BY sd.alerttime DESC NULLS LAST, sd.sensortrack DESC) as rn
            FROM sensordata sd
            WHERE sd.containlink IS NOT NULL
        )
        SELECT containlink, tempdevcount, critevents, tempstabidx, sensortrack
        FROM LatestSensor
        WHERE rn = 1
        ORDER BY containlink
        LIMIT 1;
        """
        res_sample, err_sample, _ = execute_queries([sample_query], db_name, conn)
        assert not err_sample, f"Failed to get sample container data: {err_sample}"
        assert res_sample and len(res_sample) > 0, "No suitable sample container found in sensordata"

        test_containregistry, initial_devcount, initial_crit, initial_stabidx, initial_sensortrack = res_sample[0]
        expected_initial_tss = calculate_expected_tss(initial_devcount, initial_crit, initial_stabidx)
        
        verification_query_1 = f"""
        SELECT containregistry, latest_tss, last_updated
        FROM container_tss_monitoring
        WHERE containregistry = '{test_containregistry}';
        """
        res_verify1, err_verify1, _ = execute_queries([verification_query_1], db_name, conn)
        assert not err_verify1, f"Verification query 1 failed: {err_verify1}"
        db_containreg, db_initial_tss, db_initial_ts = res_verify1[0]

        assert is_close_enough(db_initial_tss, expected_initial_tss),         f"Initial TSS mismatch for {test_containregistry}. Expected ~{expected_initial_tss}, got {db_initial_tss}"

        #update
        new_devcount = (initial_devcount or 0) + 2 
        new_crit = (initial_crit or 0) + 1
        new_stabidx = (initial_stabidx or 1.0) - 0.02
        

        update_sensor_sql = f"""
        UPDATE sensordata
        SET tempdevcount = {new_devcount},
            critevents = {new_crit},
            tempstabidx = {new_stabidx}
        WHERE sensortrack = {initial_sensortrack};
        """
        _, err_update_sensor, _ = execute_queries([update_sensor_sql], db_name, conn)
        assert not err_update_sensor, f"Failed to update source sensor data: {err_update_sensor}"

        expected_updated_tss = calculate_expected_tss(new_devcount, new_crit, new_stabidx)
        _, err_rerun, _ = execute_queries(pred_sqls, db_name, conn)
        assert not err_rerun, f"Re-running the UPSERT query failed: {err_rerun}"
        res_verify2, err_verify2, _ = execute_queries([verification_query_1], db_name, conn)
        _, db_updated_tss, db_updated_ts = res_verify2[0]
        
        assert is_close_enough(db_updated_tss, expected_updated_tss),         f"Updated TSS mismatch for {test_containregistry}. Expected ~{expected_updated_tss}, got {db_updated_tss}"
        return True

-- Intent: Update the sensordata table to flag entries with a 'High Breach' in the alertkind field
-- if the Temperature Alert (kb id 16) condition is met
UPDATE sensordata sd
-- Step 1: Set the alertkind to 'High Temp Breach'.
SET alertkind = 'High Temp Breach',
    -- Step 1a: Update the alert time to the current time.
    alerttime = NOW()
-- Step 2: Calculate Temperature Breach Severity (TBS) inline.
--         TBS = ABS(TempNowC - StoreTempC) / TempTolC * TempDevCount.
--         Handle potential division by zero or NULLs in TempTolC.
WHERE (
    CASE
        WHEN sd.temptolc IS NOT NULL AND sd.temptolc > 0 AND sd.tempnowc IS NOT NULL AND sd.storetempc IS NOT NULL AND sd.tempdevcount IS NOT NULL
        THEN (ABS(sd.tempnowc - sd.storetempc) / sd.temptolc) * sd.tempdevcount
        ELSE 0 -- Assign 0 severity if data is missing or tol is zero
    END
) > 2.0 AND sd.tempdevcount > 5-- Step 3: Filter for TBS greater than the threshold (2.0).
-- Step 4: Only update if the alert is not already set to this or another critical alert.
AND sd.alertkind NOT IN ('High Temp Breach', 'Temperature'); -- Avoid redundant updates or overwriting existing critical alerts

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
        after_query = """
        SELECT COUNT(*)
        FROM sensordata sd
        WHERE (
            CASE
                WHEN sd.temptolc IS NOT NULL AND sd.temptolc > 0 AND sd.tempnowc IS NOT NULL AND sd.storetempc IS NOT NULL AND sd.tempdevcount IS NOT NULL
                THEN (ABS(sd.tempnowc - sd.storetempc) / sd.temptolc) * sd.tempdevcount
                ELSE 0 -- Assign 0 severity if data is missing or tol is zero
            END
        ) > 2.0
        AND sd.tempdevcount > 5
        AND sd.alertkind NOT IN ('High Temp Breach', 'Temperature'); -- Check if any rows *still* meet this
        """
        res, _, _ = execute_queries([after_query], db_name, conn)

        assert res[0][0] == 0, f"Test case failed: {res[0][0]} rows still meet the condition after update"

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
        def is_close_enough(a, b, rel_tol=1e-9, abs_tol=1e-9):
            return abs(float(a) - float(b)) <= max(rel_tol * max(abs(float(a)), abs(float(b))), abs_tol)
        calculate_rcp_query = """
        SELECT vehiclereg, distdonekm, distleftkm
        FROM transportinfo
        WHERE distdonekm IS NOT NULL
          AND distleftkm IS NOT NULL
          AND (distdonekm + distleftkm) > 0 
        ORDER BY vehiclereg 
        LIMIT 1;
        """

        res, _, _ = execute_queries([calculate_rcp_query], db_name, conn)
        assert len(res) > 0, "No records found in transportinfo table"
        vehicle_reg, distdonekm, distleftkm = res[0]
        expected_rcp = (distdonekm / (distdonekm + distleftkm)) * 100

        use_function = f"""
        SELECT calculate_rcp('{vehicle_reg}');
        """
        res, _, _ = execute_queries([use_function], db_name, conn)
        assert is_close_enough(res[0][0], expected_rcp), f"Test case failed: Expected {expected_rcp}, got {res[0][0]}"
        return True

sol_sql:
-- Intent: Update the containerflag to 'Active-Efficient' for containers that are currently 'Active'
--         and meet the 'Efficient Container' criteria (SER > 0.8 and latest TSS > 0.9),
--         calculating latest TSS directly without a separate monitoring table.
WITH LatestSensorData AS (
    -- Step 1: Identify the latest sensor reading for each container using ROW_NUMBER.
    SELECT
        sd.containlink,
        sd.tempdevcount,
        sd.critevents,
        sd.tempstabidx,
        -- Assign row number 1 to the latest record per container
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY sd.containlink ORDER BY sd.alerttime DESC NULLS LAST, sd.sensortrack DESC) as rn
    FROM
        sensordata sd
), ContainerMetrics AS (
    -- Step 2: Join container, vaccine details, and the latest sensor data.
    --         Calculate SER and TSS using the latest sensor values.
    SELECT
        c.containregistry,
        c.containflag AS current_flag, -- Select current flag for filtering later
        -- Step 2a: Calculate Storage Efficiency Ratio (SER).
        CASE
            WHEN c.volliters IS NOT NULL AND c.volliters > 0 AND vd.vialtally IS NOT NULL
            THEN (vd.vialtally * 10.0) / c.volliters
            ELSE 0 -- Assign 0 efficiency if volume is zero/null or vial tally is null
        END AS calculated_ser,
        -- Step 2b: Calculate Temperature Stability Score (TSS) using latest sensor data.
        CASE
            WHEN lsd.containlink IS NOT NULL THEN -- Check if latest sensor data exists
                (1.0 - COALESCE(lsd.tempdevcount, 0) / 100.0)
                * (1.0 - COALESCE(lsd.critevents, 0) / 10.0)
                * COALESCE(lsd.tempstabidx, 1.0)
            ELSE NULL -- Cannot calculate TSS if no sensor data found
        END AS calculated_tss
    FROM
        container c
    -- Step 2c: Join with vaccine details for SER.
    JOIN
        vaccinedetails vd ON c.containregistry = vd.containvac
    -- Step 2d: LEFT Join with the latest sensor data CTE result (only rn=1).
    -- Using LEFT JOIN ensures we still process containers even if they have no sensor data yet
    -- (though the final WHERE clause will likely filter them out if TSS is required).
    LEFT JOIN
        LatestSensorData lsd ON c.containregistry = lsd.containlink AND lsd.rn = 1
)
-- Step 3: Update the container table.
UPDATE container c
-- Step 4: Set the containflag to 'Active-Efficient'.
SET containflag = 'Active-Efficient'
-- Step 5: Join with the CTE containing calculated metrics.
FROM ContainerMetrics cm
WHERE c.containregistry = cm.containregistry
-- Step 6: Apply the update only where efficiency criteria are met (SER > 0.8 AND TSS > 0.9).
  AND cm.calculated_ser > 0.8
  AND cm.calculated_tss IS NOT NULL -- Ensure TSS could be calculated
  AND cm.calculated_tss > 0.9
-- Step 7: Only update containers that are currently 'Active'.
  AND cm.current_flag = 'Active'; -- Use the flag selected in the CTE
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    after_query = """
    WITH LatestSensorData AS (
        SELECT
            sd.containlink,
            sd.tempdevcount,
            sd.critevents,
            sd.tempstabidx,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY sd.containlink ORDER BY sd.alerttime DESC NULLS LAST, sd.sensortrack DESC) as rn
        FROM
            sensordata sd
    ), ContainerMetrics AS (
        SELECT
            c.containregistry,
            c.containflag AS current_flag,
            CASE
                WHEN c.volliters IS NOT NULL AND c.volliters > 0 AND vd.vialtally IS NOT NULL
                THEN (vd.vialtally * 10.0) / c.volliters
                ELSE 0
            END AS calculated_ser,
            CASE
                WHEN lsd.containlink IS NOT NULL THEN
                    (1.0 - COALESCE(lsd.tempdevcount, 0) / 100.0)
                    * (1.0 - COALESCE(lsd.critevents, 0) / 10.0)
                    * COALESCE(lsd.tempstabidx, 1.0)
                ELSE NULL
            END AS calculated_tss
        FROM
            container c
        JOIN
            vaccinedetails vd ON c.containregistry = vd.containvac
        LEFT JOIN
            LatestSensorData lsd ON c.containregistry = lsd.containlink AND lsd.rn = 1
    )
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS remaining_matches
    FROM ContainerMetrics cm
    WHERE
        cm.calculated_ser > 0.8
    AND cm.calculated_tss IS NOT NULL
    AND cm.calculated_tss > 0.9
    AND cm.current_flag = 'Active';
    """
    res, _, _ = execute_queries([after_query], db_name, conn)

    assert res[0][0] == 0, f"Test case failed: {res[0][0]} rows still meet the condition after update"

sol_sql:
WITH SafetyScores AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate SIS (ID 34) by summing safety incident fields from JSONB, handling nulls with COALESCE.
    -- Uses JSONB operator (->>), COALESCE for null handling, and INNER JOIN to ensure valid records.
    SELECT 
        ps.effectivenessrobot,
        SUM(
            COALESCE((safety_metrics->>'overloads')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((safety_metrics->>'collisions')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((safety_metrics->>'emergency_stops')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((safety_metrics->>'speed_violations')::int, 0)
        ) AS sis
    FROM performance_and_safety ps
    GROUP BY ps.effectivenessrobot
),
RecentFaults AS (
    -- Step 2: Rank maintenance records by recency (upkeepduedays) using a window function to find recent faults.
    -- Uses ROW_NUMBER() window function and LEFT JOIN to include robots with no maintenance records.
    SELECT 
        mf.upkeeprobot,
        mf.faultpredscore,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY mf.upkeeprobot ORDER BY mf.upkeepduedays ASC) AS rn
    FROM maintenance_and_fault mf
    WHERE mf.upkeepduedays IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    s.effectivenessrobot,
    s.sis,
    rf.faultpredscore AS recent_fault_score
FROM SafetyScores s
INNER JOIN RecentFaults rf ON s.effectivenessrobot = rf.upkeeprobot
WHERE s.sis > 20  -- High Safety Concern (ID 44)
AND rf.rn = 1     -- Most recent maintenance record
AND rf.faultpredscore > 0.5 ORDER BY s.sis DESC LIMIT 100

WITH Degradation AS (
    -- Step 1: Compute JDI (ID 32) and MJT (ID 2) for Joint Health Risk (ID 42).
    -- Uses GREATEST for MJT and INNER JOIN for valid data.
    SELECT 
        jc.jcdetref,
        SUM(
            (jc.j1tempval / GREATEST(jc.j1tempval, jc.j2tempval, jc.j3tempval, jc.j4tempval, jc.j5tempval, jc.j6tempval)) +
            (jc.j2tempval / GREATEST(jc.j1tempval, jc.j2tempval, jc.j3tempval, jc.j4tempval, jc.j5tempval, jc.j6tempval)) +
            (jc.j3tempval / GREATEST(jc.j1tempval, jc.j2tempval, jc.j3tempval, jc.j4tempval, jc.j5tempval, jc.j6tempval)) +
            (jc.j4tempval / GREATEST(jc.j1tempval, jc.j2tempval, jc.j3tempval, jc.j4tempval, jc.j5tempval, jc.j6tempval)) +
            (jc.j5tempval / GREATEST(jc.j1tempval, jc.j2tempval, jc.j3tempval, jc.j4tempval, jc.j5tempval, jc.j6tempval)) +
            (jc.j6tempval / GREATEST(jc.j1tempval, jc.j2tempval, jc.j3tempval, jc.j4tempval, jc.j5tempval, jc.j6tempval)) +
            jc.j1vibval + jc.j2vibval + jc.j3vibval + jc.j4vibval + jc.j5vibval + jc.j6vibval
        ) / (COUNT(*) * 6) AS jdi,
        MAX(GREATEST(jc.j1tempval, jc.j2tempval, jc.j3tempval, jc.j4tempval, jc.j5tempval, jc.j6tempval)) AS mjt
    FROM joint_condition jc
    GROUP BY jc.jcdetref
),
OperatingHours AS (
    -- Step 2: Compute TOH (ID 7) as maximum operating hours.
    -- Uses MAX aggregation and LEFT JOIN to include all robots.
    SELECT 
        o.operbotdetref,
        MAX(o.totopshrval) AS toh
    FROM operation o
    GROUP BY o.operbotdetref
)
SELECT 
    d.jcdetref,
    d.jdi,
    d.mjt,
    oh.toh,
    REGR_SLOPE(d.jdi, oh.toh) OVER () AS jdi_toh_slope
FROM Degradation d
INNER JOIN OperatingHours oh ON d.jcdetref = oh.operbotdetref
WHERE d.jdi > 1.5 AND d.mjt > 65  -- Joint Health Risk (ID 42)
  AND oh.toh > 5000               -- Additional filter for regression analysis
ORDER BY d.jdi DESC
LIMIT 100

WITH SafetyIncidents AS (
    -- Step 1: Compute SIS (ID 34) with JSONB field extraction.
    -- Uses ->> operator for safe JSONB access.
    SELECT 
        ps.effectivenessrobot,
        SUM(
            COALESCE((ps.safety_metrics->>'overloads')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((ps.safety_metrics->>'collisions')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((ps.safety_metrics->>'emergency_stops')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((ps.safety_metrics->>'speed_violations')::int, 0)
        ) AS sis
    FROM performance_and_safety ps
    GROUP BY ps.effectivenessrobot
)
SELECT 
    rd.bottypeval AS robot_type,
    AVG(si.sis) AS avg_sis,
    MAX(si.sis) AS max_sis
FROM SafetyIncidents si
INNER JOIN robot_details rd ON si.effectivenessrobot = rd.botdetreg
WHERE si.sis > 20  -- High Safety Concern (ID 44)
GROUP BY rd.bottypeval
ORDER BY avg_sis DESC
LIMIT 100

WITH controller_metrics AS (
    -- Calculate CSI as per definition
    SELECT systemoverseerrobot,
           AVG((controller_metrics->>'load_value')::float + 
               (controller_metrics->>'thermal_level')::float) as csi
    FROM system_controller
    GROUP BY systemoverseerrobot
),
operation_count AS (
    -- Calculate NO as per knowledge bank definition
    SELECT operbotdetref,
           COUNT(*) as operation_count
    FROM operation
    GROUP BY operbotdetref
)
SELECT rd.botdetreg,
       cm.csi,
       oc.operation_count,
       CASE WHEN cm.csi > 100 AND oc.operation_count > 2 THEN 'HIGH RISK'
            ELSE 'NORMAL' END as controller_status
FROM robot_details rd
JOIN controller_metrics cm ON rd.botdetreg = cm.systemoverseerrobot
JOIN operation_count oc ON rd.botdetreg = oc.operbotdetref
ORDER BY cm.csi DESC
LIMIT 100

WITH fault_metrics AS (
    SELECT upkeeprobot,
           SUM(faultpredscore * (1.0/(1 + upkeepduedays))) / 
           SUM(1.0/(1 + upkeepduedays)) as wfps,  -- WFPS (ID: 30)
           MIN(rulhours) as mrul  -- MRUL (ID: 6)
    FROM maintenance_and_fault
    GROUP BY upkeeprobot
),
operation_stress AS (
    SELECT operbotdetref,
           MAX(totopshrval) as toh,  -- TOH (ID: 7)
           COUNT(*) as operation_count  -- NO (ID: 8)
    FROM operation
    GROUP BY operbotdetref
)
SELECT rd.botdetreg,
       fm.wfps,
       fm.mrul,
       os.toh,
       os.operation_count,
       CASE WHEN fm.wfps > 0.6 AND fm.mrul < 200 THEN 'CRITICAL'  -- Critical Maintenance Priority (ID: 40)
            WHEN fm.wfps > 0.4 OR fm.mrul < 500 THEN 'WARNING'
            ELSE 'NORMAL' END as maintenance_priority
FROM robot_details rd
JOIN fault_metrics fm ON rd.botdetreg = fm.upkeeprobot
JOIN operation_stress os ON rd.botdetreg = os.operbotdetref
WHERE os.toh > 1000
ORDER BY fm.wfps DESC, fm.mrul ASC LIMIT 100

WITH tool_metrics AS (
    SELECT effectivenessrobot,
           AVG(toolwearpct) as avg_wear,
           SUM(toolwearpct)/NULLIF(SUM(tc.progcyclecount), 0) as twr  -- TWR (ID: 37)
    FROM performance_and_safety ps
    JOIN operation tc ON ps.effectivenessrobot = tc.operbotdetref
    GROUP BY effectivenessrobot
),
cycle_metrics AS (
    SELECT operbotdetref,
           SUM(progcyclecount) as tpc,  -- TPC (ID: 9)
           AVG(cycletimesecval) as avg_cycle_time
    FROM operation
    GROUP BY operbotdetref
)
SELECT rd.botdetreg,
       tm.avg_wear,
       tm.twr,
       cm.tpc,
       cm.avg_cycle_time,
       CASE WHEN tm.twr > 0.001 AND cm.tpc > 10000 THEN 'URGENT'  -- Tool Replacement Status (ID: 47)
            WHEN tm.twr > 0.0005 OR tm.avg_wear > 75 THEN 'WARNING'
            ELSE 'NORMAL' END as tool_status
FROM robot_details rd
JOIN tool_metrics tm ON rd.botdetreg = tm.effectivenessrobot
JOIN cycle_metrics cm ON rd.botdetreg = cm.operbotdetref
ORDER BY tm.twr DESC, cm.tpc DESC LIMIT 100

WITH cycle_metrics AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate cycle efficiency metrics
    SELECT 
        o.operbotdetref,
        SUM(o.progcyclecount) as tpc,
        AVG(o.cycletimesecval) as avg_cycle_time,
        SUM(o.progcyclecount)/NULLIF(SUM(o.cycletimesecval), 0) as oce,
        array_agg(DISTINCT o.currprogval) as unique_programs
    FROM operation o
    GROUP BY o.operbotdetref
),
temporal_efficiency AS (
    -- Step 2: Analyze efficiency trends over time
    SELECT 
        cm.operbotdetref,
        cm.oce,
        cm.tpc,
        cm.avg_cycle_time,
        cardinality(cm.unique_programs) as program_count,
        FIRST_VALUE(o.cycletimesecval) OVER (
            PARTITION BY o.operbotdetref 
            ORDER BY rr.rects DESC
        ) as latest_cycle_time
    FROM cycle_metrics cm
    JOIN operation o ON cm.operbotdetref = o.operbotdetref
    JOIN robot_record rr ON o.operrecref = rr.recreg
)
SELECT 
    rd.botdetreg,
    rd.modelseriesval,
    te.oce,
    te.tpc,
    te.avg_cycle_time,
    te.program_count,
    te.latest_cycle_time,
    CASE 
        WHEN te.oce < 100 AND te.tpc > 500000 THEN 'Low Efficiency'
        WHEN te.oce < 150 OR te.tpc > 300000 THEN 'Medium Efficiency'
        ELSE 'High Efficiency'
    END as efficiency_category,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (
        PARTITION BY rd.modelseriesval 
        ORDER BY te.oce DESC
    ) as efficiency_rank_in_model
FROM robot_details rd
JOIN temporal_efficiency te ON rd.botdetreg = te.operbotdetref
WHERE te.tpc > 100000
ORDER BY te.oce DESC, te.tpc DESC LIMIT 100

WITH inefficient_robots AS (
    SELECT 
        rd.botdetreg AS robot_id,
        op.apptypeval AS application_type,
        rd.modelseriesval AS model_series,
        -- Calculate Energy Efficiency Ratio (EER)
        SUM(ps.energyusekwhval) / MAX(op.totopshrval) AS eer
    FROM robot_details rd
    JOIN operation op ON rd.botdetreg = op.operbotdetref
    JOIN performance_and_safety ps ON rd.botdetreg = ps.effectivenessrobot
    GROUP BY rd.botdetreg, op.apptypeval, rd.modelseriesval
    HAVING SUM(ps.energyusekwhval) / MAX(op.totopshrval) > 0.01 
       AND MAX(op.totopshrval) > 1000
),
eer_ranks AS (
    SELECT 
        application_type,
        robot_id,
        eer,
        -- Calculate EER Rank within each application type
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY application_type ORDER BY eer DESC) AS eer_rank
    FROM inefficient_robots
)
SELECT 
    application_type,
    AVG(eer) AS average_eer,
    MAX(eer_rank) AS worst_eer_rank
FROM eer_ranks
GROUP BY application_type ORDER BY average_eer DESC LIMIT 100

WITH PositionError AS (
    -- Compute APE per robot
    SELECT 
        ad.actdetref,
        rd.ctrltypeval,
        AVG(ad.poserrmmval) AS ape  -- ID 3, APE
    FROM actuation_data ad
    JOIN robot_details rd ON ad.actdetref = rd.botdetreg
    GROUP BY ad.actdetref, rd.ctrltypeval
),
FaultScore AS (
    -- Compute RFPS from most recent maintenance record
    SELECT 
        mf.upkeeprobot,
        MAX(mf.faultpredscore) FILTER (WHERE mf.upkeepduedays = (
            SELECT MIN(upkeepduedays) FROM maintenance_and_fault mf2 
            WHERE mf2.upkeeprobot = mf.upkeeprobot
        )) AS rfps  -- ID 5, RFPS
    FROM maintenance_and_fault mf
    GROUP BY mf.upkeeprobot
),
RobotAge AS (
    -- Compute robot age in years
    SELECT 
        rd.botdetreg,
        (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (rr.rects - rd.instdateval)) / (365.25 * 24 * 3600)) AS ray  -- ID 0, RAY
    FROM robot_details rd
    JOIN robot_record rr ON rd.botdetreg = rr.recreg
),
Ranked AS (
    -- Rank APE within controller type
    SELECT 
        pe.actdetref,
        pe.ctrltypeval,
        pe.ape,
        PERCENT_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY pe.ctrltypeval ORDER BY pe.ape DESC) AS ape_rank 
    FROM PositionError pe
)
SELECT 
    r.ctrltypeval AS controller_type,
    AVG(r.ape) AS avg_ape,  -- ID 3, APE
    MAX(r.ape_rank) AS max_ape_rank, 
    AVG(COALESCE(fs.rfps, 0)) AS avg_rfps,  -- ID 5, RFPS
    COUNT(DISTINCT r.actdetref) AS robot_count
FROM Ranked r
LEFT JOIN FaultScore fs ON r.actdetref = fs.upkeeprobot
INNER JOIN RobotAge ra ON r.actdetref = ra.botdetreg
WHERE r.ape >= 0.1  -- ID 13, Precision Category Low Precision
AND COALESCE(fs.rfps, 0) > 0.5  -- ID 15, High Fault Risk (adjusted) 
AND ra.ray > 1  -- ID 0, RAY
GROUP BY r.ctrltypeval
ORDER BY max_ape_rank DESC
LIMIT 50

WITH program_metrics AS (
    -- Calculate program-specific metrics
    SELECT 
        o.operbotdetref,
        o.currprogval,
        COUNT(*) as execution_count,
        AVG(o.cycletimesecval) as avg_cycle_time,      -- Knowledge #51: Average Cycle Time
        STDDEV(o.cycletimesecval) as cycle_time_std,   -- Used for variation analysis
        SUM(o.progcyclecount) as total_cycles,         -- Knowledge #9: TPC (Total Program Cycles)
        SUM(o.progcyclecount)/NULLIF(SUM(o.cycletimesecval), 0) as program_oce  -- Knowledge #33: OCE per program
    FROM operation o
    GROUP BY o.operbotdetref, o.currprogval
),
robot_efficiency AS (
    -- Calculate robot-level efficiency metrics
    SELECT 
        pm.operbotdetref,
        COUNT(DISTINCT pm.currprogval) as program_count,
        AVG(pm.avg_cycle_time) as overall_cycle_time,   -- Knowledge #51: Average Cycle Time
        SUM(pm.total_cycles) as total_program_cycles,   -- Knowledge #9: Aggregated TPC
        SUM(pm.total_cycles)/NULLIF(SUM(pm.total_cycles * pm.avg_cycle_time), 0) as robot_oce,  -- Knowledge #33: OCE per robot
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT pm.currprogval) as programs_used
    FROM program_metrics pm
    GROUP BY pm.operbotdetref
),
program_ranking AS (
    -- Rank programs by efficiency using OCE instead of just cycle time
    SELECT 
        pm.currprogval,
        AVG(pm.program_oce) as avg_program_oce,        -- Knowledge #33: Average OCE per program
        COUNT(DISTINCT pm.operbotdetref) as robot_count,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AVG(pm.program_oce) DESC) as efficiency_rank  -- Now properly using OCE
    FROM program_metrics pm
    GROUP BY pm.currprogval
)
SELECT 
    rd.modelseriesval,
    COUNT(DISTINCT re.operbotdetref) as robot_count,
    ROUND(AVG(re.robot_oce)::numeric, 2) as avg_oce,   -- Knowledge #33: Average OCE
    ROUND(AVG(re.program_count)::numeric, 2) as avg_programs_per_robot,
    ROUND(AVG(re.total_program_cycles)::numeric, 0) as avg_total_cycles,  -- Knowledge #9
    jsonb_build_object(
        'most_efficient_program', (
            SELECT currprogval 
            FROM program_ranking 
            WHERE efficiency_rank = 1    -- Now based on OCE ranking
            LIMIT 1
        ),
        'avg_program_efficiency', (
            SELECT ROUND(AVG(avg_program_oce)::numeric, 2)  -- Knowledge #33
            FROM program_ranking
        )
    ) as efficiency_metrics,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (
        WHERE re.robot_oce < 100 AND re.total_program_cycles > 500000  -- Knowledge #43: Cycle Efficiency Category
    ) as low_efficiency_robots
FROM robot_details rd
JOIN robot_efficiency re ON rd.botdetreg = re.operbotdetref
GROUP BY rd.modelseriesval
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY avg_oce DESC    -- Knowledge #33: Sorting by OCE instead of cycle time
LIMIT 50

ALTER TABLE robot_details
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS precision_category character varying(20);

-- Step 2: Calculate the Average Position Error (APE) for each robot.
WITH RobotAPE AS (
    -- Step 2a: Compute the average 'poserrmmval' for each robot from actuation_data.
    SELECT
        actdetref AS robot_id,
        AVG(poserrmmval) AS avg_pos_error -- KB #3: Average Position Error
    FROM actuation_data
    WHERE actdetref IS NOT NULL AND poserrmmval IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY actdetref
)
-- Step 3: Update the new 'precision_category' column based on the calculated APE.
UPDATE robot_details rd
SET precision_category =
    -- Step 3a: Use a CASE statement to assign the category based on APE thresholds (KB #13).
    CASE
        WHEN r_ape.avg_pos_error < 0.1 THEN 'High Precision'
        WHEN r_ape.avg_pos_error < 0.5 THEN 'Medium Precision'
        ELSE 'Low Precision'
    END
-- Step 3b: Join robot_details with the calculated APE values.
FROM RobotAPE r_ape
WHERE rd.botdetreg = r_ape.robot_id;

WITH RobotSafetyIncidents AS (

        effectivenessrobot AS robot_id,
        SUM(
            COALESCE((safety_metrics->>'overloads')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((safety_metrics->>'collisions')::int, 0) +
            COALESCE((safety_metrics->>'emergency_stops')::int, 0) +

        ) AS safety_incident_score -- KB #34: Safety Incident Score

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS robot_maintenance_priority (
    robot_id character varying PRIMARY KEY,
    priority_level character varying,
    calculated_wfps numeric,
    calculated_mrul integer,
    last_updated timestamp DEFAULT current_timestamp,
    FOREIGN KEY (robot_id) REFERENCES robot_details(botdetreg)
);

-- Step 2: Calculate WFPS and MRUL for each robot using CTEs.
WITH RobotFaultData AS (
    -- Step 2a: Select fault prediction score and due days for each robot from maintenance_and_fault.
    SELECT
        upkeeprobot AS robot_id,
        faultpredscore,
        upkeepduedays,
        rulhours
    FROM maintenance_and_fault
    WHERE upkeeprobot IS NOT NULL AND faultpredscore IS NOT NULL AND upkeepduedays IS NOT NULL AND rulhours IS NOT NULL
),
WeightedScores AS (
    -- Step 2b: Calculate the weight (1 / (1 + upkeepduedays)) and weighted score for each record.
    SELECT
        robot_id,
        faultpredscore * (1.0 / (1.0 + upkeepduedays)) AS weighted_score,
        (1.0 / (1.0 + upkeepduedays)) AS weight,
        rulhours
    FROM RobotFaultData
),
RobotMetrics AS (
    -- Step 2c: Aggregate weighted scores and weights per robot to calculate WFPS, and find the minimum RUL (MRUL).
    SELECT
        robot_id,
        SUM(weighted_score) / SUM(weight) AS wfps, -- KB #30: Weighted Fault Prediction Score
        MIN(rulhours) AS mrul -- KB #6: Minimum Remaining Useful Life
    FROM WeightedScores
    GROUP BY robot_id
)
-- Step 3: Insert or update the robot_maintenance_priority table.
INSERT INTO robot_maintenance_priority (robot_id, priority_level, calculated_wfps, calculated_mrul, last_updated)
SELECT
    rm.robot_id,
    -- Step 3a: Determine the priority level based on WFPS and MRUL thresholds (KB #40).
    CASE
        WHEN rm.wfps > 0.6 AND rm.mrul < 500 THEN 'CRITICAL'
        WHEN rm.wfps > 0.4 OR rm.mrul < 500 THEN 'WARNING'
        ELSE 'NORMAL'
    END AS priority_level,
    rm.wfps,
    rm.mrul,
    current_timestamp
FROM RobotMetrics rm
-- Step 3b: If a robot_id already exists, update its priority level, metrics, and timestamp.
ON CONFLICT (robot_id) DO UPDATE SET
    priority_level = EXCLUDED.priority_level,
    calculated_wfps = EXCLUDED.calculated_wfps,
    calculated_mrul = EXCLUDED.calculated_mrul,
    last_updated = EXCLUDED.last_updated;

-- Example SELECT to verify (Optional)
-- SELECT * FROM robot_maintenance_priority ORDER BY calculated_wfps DESC LIMIT 5;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    def is_close_enough(a, b, rel_tol=1e-9, abs_tol=1e-9):
        return abs(float(a) - float(b)) <= max(rel_tol * max(abs(float(a)), abs(float(b))), abs_tol)
    sample_query = """
    WITH RobotFaultData AS (
        SELECT
            upkeeprobot AS robot_id,
            faultpredscore,
            upkeepduedays,
            rulhours
        FROM maintenance_and_fault
        WHERE upkeeprobot IS NOT NULL AND faultpredscore IS NOT NULL AND upkeepduedays IS NOT NULL AND rulhours IS NOT NULL
    ),
    WeightedScores AS (
        -- Step 2b: Calculate the weight (1 / (1 + upkeepduedays)) and weighted score for each record.
        SELECT
            robot_id,
            faultpredscore * (1.0 / (1.0 + upkeepduedays)) AS weighted_score,
            (1.0 / (1.0 + upkeepduedays)) AS weight,
            rulhours
        FROM RobotFaultData
    ),
    RobotMetrics AS (
        SELECT
            robot_id,
            SUM(weighted_score) / SUM(weight) AS wfps, 
            MIN(rulhours) AS mrul 
        FROM WeightedScores
        GROUP BY robot_id
    )
    SELECT
        rm.robot_id,
        CASE
            WHEN rm.wfps > 0.6 AND rm.mrul < 500 THEN 'CRITICAL'
            WHEN rm.wfps > 0.4 OR rm.mrul < 500 THEN 'WARNING'
            ELSE 'NORMAL'
        END AS priority_level,
        rm.wfps,
        rm.mrul,
        current_timestamp
    FROM RobotMetrics rm
    LIMIT 1
    """
    res, _, _ = execute_queries([sample_query], db_name, conn)
    assert len(res) > 0, "No data found"
    wanted_robot_id, expected_priority_level, expected_wfps, expected_mrul, _ = res[0]
    validation_query = f"""
        SELECT calculated_wfps, calculated_mrul, priority_level
        FROM robot_maintenance_priority
        WHERE robot_id = '{wanted_robot_id}';
    """
    res, _, _ = execute_queries([validation_query], db_name, conn)
    assert len(res) > 0, "Error: No match found"
    actual_wfps, actual_mrul, actual_priority_level = res[0]
    assert is_close_enough(actual_wfps, expected_wfps), f"Test case failed: Expected {expected_wfps}, got {actual_wfps}"
    assert is_close_enough(actual_mrul, expected_mrul), f"Test case failed: Expected {expected_mrul}, got {actual_mrul}"
    assert actual_priority_level == expected_priority_level, f"Test case failed: Expected {expected_priority_level}, got {actual_priority_level}"
    update_query = f"""
    UPDATE maintenance_and_fault
    SET faultpredscore = 0.9, upkeepduedays = 0, rulhours = 1
    WHERE upkeeprobot = '{wanted_robot_id}';
    """
    execute_queries([update_query], db_name, conn)
    execute_queries(pred_sqls, db_name, conn)

    res, _, _ = execute_queries([validation_query], db_name, conn)
    assert len(res) > 0, "Error: No match after update"
    _, _, updated_priority_level = res[0]
    assert updated_priority_level == 'CRITICAL', f"Update test failed: Expected CRITICAL, got {updated_priority_level}"
    return True

WITH OldRobots AS (
    -- Step 1a: Find robots older than 2 years (KB #10). Calculate age using robot installation date and current date.
    SELECT
        rd.botdetreg
    FROM robot_details rd
    JOIN robot_record rr ON rd.botdetreg = rr.recreg -- Assumes recreg links to details
    WHERE rd.instdateval IS NOT NULL AND AGE(CURRENT_DATE, rd.instdateval) >= INTERVAL '2 years' -- KB #0: RAY
),
RecordsToDelete AS (
    -- Step 1b: Select upkeepactuation from maintenance_and_fault for old robots with a low fault prediction score.
    SELECT mf.upkeepactuation
    FROM maintenance_and_fault mf
    JOIN OldRobots oro ON mf.upkeeprobot = oro.botdetreg
    WHERE mf.faultpredscore < 0.05 
)
-- Step 2: Delete the identified records from the maintenance_and_fault table.
DELETE FROM maintenance_and_fault mf
USING RecordsToDelete rtd
WHERE mf.upkeepactuation = rtd.upkeepactuation
RETURNING mf.upkeepactuation;

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS robot_model_performance_summary AS
WITH RobotBaseMetrics AS (
    -- Step 1a: Calculate Average Position Error (APE) and Average TCP Speed (ATCS) per robot.
    SELECT
        actdetref AS robot_id,
        AVG(poserrmmval) AS avg_pos_error, -- KB #3: Average Position Error
        AVG(tcpspeedval) AS avg_tcp_speed -- KB #4: Average TCP Speed
    FROM actuation_data
    WHERE actdetref IS NOT NULL AND poserrmmval IS NOT NULL AND tcpspeedval IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY actdetref
),
RobotOperationHours AS (
    -- Step 1b: Find the Maximum Total Operating Hours (TOH) per robot.
    SELECT
        operbotdetref AS robot_id,
        MAX(totopshrval) AS max_ops_hours -- KB #7: Total Operating Hours
    FROM operation
    WHERE operbotdetref IS NOT NULL AND totopshrval IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY operbotdetref
)
-- Step 1c: Join robot details with calculated metrics and aggregate by model series.
SELECT
    rd.modelseriesval,
    COUNT(DISTINCT rd.botdetreg) AS robot_count,
    ROUND(AVG(rbm.avg_pos_error)::numeric, 3) AS model_avg_pos_error,
    ROUND(AVG(rbm.avg_tcp_speed)::numeric, 3) AS model_avg_tcp_speed,
    ROUND(AVG(roh.max_ops_hours)::numeric, 3) AS model_avg_max_ops_hours
FROM robot_details rd
LEFT JOIN RobotBaseMetrics rbm ON rd.botdetreg = rbm.robot_id
LEFT JOIN RobotOperationHours roh ON rd.botdetreg = roh.robot_id
WHERE rd.modelseriesval IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY rd.modelseriesval;

-- Step 2: Refresh the Materialized View (Example of controlling refresh)
REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW robot_model_performance_summary;

-- Example SELECT to verify (Optional)
-- SELECT * FROM robot_model_performance_summary ORDER BY model_avg_pos_error ASC;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
        def is_close_enough(a, b, rel_tol=1e-9, abs_tol=1e-9):
            return abs(float(a) - float(b)) <= max(rel_tol * max(abs(float(a)), abs(float(b))), abs_tol)

        # === Step 1: Check View Existence ===
        view_exists_query = """
            SELECT 1 FROM pg_matviews WHERE matviewname = 'robot_model_performance_summary';
        """
        res, _, _ = execute_queries([view_exists_query], db_name, conn)
        assert len(res) > 0 and res[0][0] == 1, "Test Case Failed: Materialized view 'robot_model_performance_summary' does not exist."

        select_one_row_query = """
            SELECT
                modelseriesval,
                robot_count,
                model_avg_pos_error,
                model_avg_tcp_speed,
                model_avg_max_ops_hours
            FROM robot_model_performance_summary
            ORDER BY modelseriesval 
            LIMIT 1;
        """
        res, _, _ = execute_queries([select_one_row_query], db_name, conn)
        assert len(res) > 0, "Test Case Failed: Materialized view 'robot_model_performance_summary' is empty."

        actual_model_series, actual_robot_count, actual_ape, actual_atcs, actual_toh = res[0]

        expected_values_query = f"""
        WITH RobotBaseMetrics AS (
            -- Calculate APE and ATCS per robot *within the specific model series*
            SELECT
                ad.actdetref AS robot_id,
                AVG(ad.poserrmmval) AS avg_pos_error,
                AVG(ad.tcpspeedval) AS avg_tcp_speed
            FROM actuation_data ad
            JOIN robot_details rd_filter ON ad.actdetref = rd_filter.botdetreg 
            WHERE rd_filter.modelseriesval = '{actual_model_series}' 
            AND ad.actdetref IS NOT NULL AND ad.poserrmmval IS NOT NULL AND ad.tcpspeedval IS NOT NULL
            GROUP BY ad.actdetref
        ),
        RobotOperationHours AS (
            SELECT
                op.operbotdetref AS robot_id,
                MAX(op.totopshrval) AS max_ops_hours
            FROM operation op
            JOIN robot_details rd_filter ON op.operbotdetref = rd_filter.botdetreg 
            WHERE rd_filter.modelseriesval = '{actual_model_series}' 
            AND op.operbotdetref IS NOT NULL AND op.totopshrval IS NOT NULL
            GROUP BY op.operbotdetref
        )
        SELECT
            COUNT(DISTINCT rd.botdetreg) AS expected_robot_count,
            ROUND(AVG(rbm.avg_pos_error)::numeric, 3) AS expected_model_avg_pos_error,
            ROUND(AVG(rbm.avg_tcp_speed)::numeric, 3) AS expected_model_avg_tcp_speed,
            ROUND(AVG(roh.max_ops_hours)::numeric, 3) AS expected_model_avg_max_ops_hours
        FROM robot_details rd
        -- LEFT JOIN ensures we consider all robots of the model series, even if they lack metrics (matching view logic)
        LEFT JOIN RobotBaseMetrics rbm ON rd.botdetreg = rbm.robot_id
        LEFT JOIN RobotOperationHours roh ON rd.botdetreg = roh.robot_id
        WHERE rd.modelseriesval = '{actual_model_series}'; -- Final filter for the specific model series
        """
        res, _, _ = execute_queries([expected_values_query], db_name, conn)
        assert len(res) > 0, f"Test Case Setup Error: Could not calculate expected values for model series '{actual_model_series}'."

        expected_robot_count, expected_ape, expected_atcs, expected_toh = res[0]

        assert actual_robot_count == expected_robot_count,             f"Test case failed: Robot count mismatch for '{actual_model_series}'. Expected {expected_robot_count}, got {actual_robot_count}"

        assert is_close_enough(actual_ape, expected_ape),             f"Test case failed: Model APE mismatch for '{actual_model_series}'. Expected {expected_ape}, got {actual_ape}"

        assert is_close_enough(actual_atcs, expected_atcs),             f"Test case failed: Model ATCS mismatch for '{actual_model_series}'. Expected {expected_atcs}, got {actual_atcs}"

        assert is_close_enough(actual_toh, expected_toh),             f"Test case failed: Model TOH mismatch for '{actual_model_series}'. Expected {expected_toh}, got {actual_toh}"

        return True

-- Intent: Identify distribution hubs with inefficient resource utilization based on RUR calculation (Knowledge ID 10, 50)
-- Uses advanced functions: JSON functions, conditional expressions

-- Step 1: Calculate RUR (Resource Utilization Ratio) for each hub
-- RUR = (hubutilpct/100) * (storecapm3/(storeavailm3 + 1))
SELECT 
    h.hubregistry,
    (h.hubutilpct / 100.0) * (h.storecapm3 / (h.storeavailm3 + 1)) AS resource_utilization_ratio,
    CASE 
        WHEN (h.hubutilpct / 100.0) * (h.storecapm3 / (h.storeavailm3 + 1)) > 5 THEN 'High Utilization'
        WHEN (h.hubutilpct / 100.0) * (h.storecapm3 / (h.storeavailm3 + 1)) < 2 THEN 'Low Utilization'
        ELSE 'Moderate Utilization'
    END AS utilization_assessment
FROM 
    distributionhubs h
ORDER BY 
    resource_utilization_ratio DESC;

-- Intent: Find operations demonstrating Operational Excellence (Knowledge ID 24, 11)
-- Uses advanced functions: Window functions, JOIN operations

-- Step 1: Calculate OEI (Operational Efficiency Index) for each operation
-- Step 2: Filter operations with high delivery success rate and inventory accuracy
WITH operation_metrics AS (
    SELECT 
        o.opsregistry,
        d.affectedarea,
        t.deliverysuccessrate,
        h.invaccpct,
        (t.deliverysuccessrate / 100.0) * (1 - t.avgdeliveryhours / 24.0) * (1 + t.distributionpoints / 10.0) AS oei
    FROM 
        operations o
    JOIN 
        disasterevents d ON o.opsdistref = d.distregistry
    JOIN 
        distributionhubs h ON o.opshubref = h.hubregistry
    JOIN 
        transportation t ON o.opsdistref = t.transportdistref
)
SELECT 
    opsregistry,
    ROUND(oei::numeric, 2) AS operational_efficiency_index,
    CASE WHEN deliverysuccessrate > 90 AND invaccpct > 95 AND oei > 3 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS operational_excellence
FROM 
    operation_metrics
ORDER BY 
    oei DESC;

-- Intent: Evaluate disaster operations based on Environmental Impact Factor (Knowledge ID 16, 51)
-- Uses advanced functions: Mathematical functions, ORDER BY with expressions

-- Step 1: Calculate the Environmental Impact Factor (EIF) for each operation
-- EIF = carbontons * (1 - renewenergypct/100) + (100 - recyclepct) * 0.5
SELECT 
    e.envhealthregistry,
    d.distregistry,
    d.affectedarea,
    d.haztype,
    ROUND(
        e.carbontons * (1 - e.renewenergypct / 100.0) + (100 - e.recyclepct) * 0.5,
        2
    ) AS environmental_impact_factor,
    CASE 
        WHEN e.carbontons * (1 - e.renewenergypct / 100.0) + (100 - e.recyclepct) * 0.5 < 50 THEN 'Sustainable'
        WHEN e.carbontons * (1 - e.renewenergypct / 100.0) + (100 - e.recyclepct) * 0.5 < 100 THEN 'Moderate Impact'
        ELSE 'High Impact'
    END AS sustainability_assessment
FROM 
    environmentandhealth e
JOIN 
    disasterevents d ON e.envdistref = d.distregistry
ORDER BY 
    environmental_impact_factor;

-- Intent: Find disaster operations facing critical resource shortages as defined in KB
-- Uses KB ID 20: Critical Resource Shortage, ID 53
-- Advanced Functions: Subquery, percentage calculation

SELECT
    o.opsregistry,
    h.storecapm3,
    h.storeavailm3,
    -- Step 1: Calculate the percentage of available storage to capacity
    ROUND((h.storeavailm3 / h.storecapm3) * 100, 2) AS available_storage_percent,
    o.supplyflowstate
FROM
    operations o
JOIN
    distributionhubs h ON o.opshubref = h.hubregistry
-- Step 2: Apply the Critical Resource Shortage criteria from Knowledge ID 20
WHERE
    (h.storeavailm3 / h.storecapm3) < 0.1
    AND o.supplyflowstate IN ('Strained', 'Disrupted')
ORDER BY
    available_storage_percent;

-- Intent: Calculate Public Health Resilience Score (PHRS) for disaster areas
-- Uses KB ID 17: Public Health Resilience Score (PHRS), ID 26
-- Advanced Functions: Weighted calculations, conditional classification

SELECT
    e.envhealthregistry,
    d.distregistry,
    -- Step 1: Calculate PHRS using the formula from Knowledge ID 17
    ROUND(e.waterqualityindex * 0.4 + e.sanitationcoverage * 0.3 + e.vaccinationcoverage * 0.3, 2) AS public_health_resilience_score,
    -- Step 2: Identify areas with public health emergencies based on Knowledge ID 26
    CASE
        WHEN e.diseaserisk = 'High' 
             AND e.waterqualityindex < 50 
             AND (e.waterqualityindex * 0.4 + e.sanitationcoverage * 0.3 + e.vaccinationcoverage * 0.3) < 40
        THEN 'Public Health Emergency'
        ELSE 'Standard Health Status'
    END AS health_status
FROM
    environmentandhealth e
JOIN
    disasterevents d ON e.envdistref = d.distregistry
ORDER BY
    public_health_resilience_score;

-- Intent: Calculate Logistics Performance Metric (LPM) for transportation operations
-- Uses KB ID 15: Logistics Performance Metric (LPM), ID 29
-- Advanced Functions: Arithmetic operations, conditional expression

SELECT
    t.transportregistry,
    ROUND(
        (t.totaldeliverytons / h.hubcaptons) * 
        t.deliverysuccessrate * 
        (1 - t.vehiclebreakrate / 100.0) * 100,
    2) AS logistics_performance_metric,
    -- Step 2: Identify logistics breakdowns based on Knowledge ID 29
    CASE
        WHEN (t.totaldeliverytons / h.hubcaptons) * t.deliverysuccessrate * (1 - t.vehiclebreakrate / 100.0) * 100 < 30 
             AND t.lastmilestatus = 'Suspended' 
             AND t.vehiclebreakrate > 15 
        THEN 'Logistics Breakdown'
        ELSE 'Normal Operation'
    END AS logistics_status
FROM
    transportation t
JOIN
    disasterevents d ON t.transportdistref = d.distregistry
JOIN
    distributionhubs h ON t.transporthubref = h.hubregistry
ORDER BY
    logistics_performance_metric;

-- Intent: Calculate the Community Engagement Effectiveness (CEE) score and identify resilience opportunities
-- Uses KB ID 38: Community Engagement Effectiveness (CEE) and KB ID 46: Community Resilience Opportunity, ID 19, ID 52
-- Advanced Functions: CASE expression for mapping, Window functions for ranking, CTEs

WITH engagement_mapping AS (
    -- Step 1: Map community engagement levels to numeric values
    SELECT
        b.beneregistry,
        b.benedistref,
        b.commengagelvl,
        b.vulnerabilityreview,
        b.benefeedbackscore,
        b.distequityidx,
        c.stakeholdersatisf,
        -- Mapping commengagelvl to numeric values as defined in KB
        CASE
            WHEN b.commengagelvl = 'Low' THEN 1
            WHEN b.commengagelvl = 'Medium' THEN 2
            WHEN b.commengagelvl = 'High' THEN 3
            ELSE 0
        END AS commengage_numeric,
        -- Calculate BSI as per Knowledge ID 19
        (b.benefeedbackscore * 10) + 
        (CASE
            WHEN b.commengagelvl = 'Low' THEN 1
            WHEN b.commengagelvl = 'Medium' THEN 2
            WHEN b.commengagelvl = 'High' THEN 3
            ELSE 0
        END * 20) + 
        (b.distequityidx * 50) AS bsi
    FROM
        beneficiariesandassessments b
    JOIN
        coordinationandevaluation c ON b.benedistref = c.coorddistref AND b.beneopsref = c.coordopsref
),
cee_calculation AS (
    -- Step 2: Calculate the CEE score using the formula from Knowledge ID 38
    SELECT
        e.*,
        ROUND((e.bsi / 100) * e.commengage_numeric * ((e.stakeholdersatisf + 1) / 5), 2) AS cee,
        CASE
            -- Identify Community Resilience Builder operations as per Knowledge ID 28
            WHEN b.localcapacitygrowth = 'Active' AND b.commengagelvl = 'High' AND e.bsi > 70 
            THEN 'Community Resilience Builder'
            -- Identify Community Resilience Opportunity as per Knowledge ID 46
            WHEN (e.bsi / 100) * e.commengage_numeric * ((e.stakeholdersatisf + 1) / 5) > 2.5 
                 AND e.vulnerabilityreview = 'Complete'
                 AND NOT (b.localcapacitygrowth = 'Active' AND b.commengagelvl = 'High' AND e.bsi > 70)
            THEN 'Community Resilience Opportunity'
            ELSE 'Standard Operation'
        END AS community_status
    FROM
        engagement_mapping e
    JOIN
        beneficiariesandassessments b ON e.beneregistry = b.beneregistry
)
-- Step 3: Retrieve the final results with rankings
SELECT
    c.beneregistry,
    c.benedistref,
    c.cee,
    c.community_status,
    -- Window function to rank operations by CEE score within each community status
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.community_status ORDER BY c.cee DESC) AS rank_within_status
FROM
    cee_calculation c
ORDER BY
    c.community_status,
    c.cee DESC;

-- Intent: Calculate Financial Sustainability Ratio (FSR) for disaster operations
-- Uses KB ID 18: Financial Sustainability Ratio (FSR), ID 27
-- Advanced Functions: Mathematical operations, conditional logic

SELECT
    f.financeregistry,
    o.opsregistry,
    d.distregistry,
    -- Step 1: Calculate FSR using the formula from Knowledge ID 18
    ROUND(
        (f.donorcommitmentsusd / f.budgetallotusd) * 
        (1 - f.fundsutilpct / 100) - 
        (f.resourcegapsusd / f.budgetallotusd),
    2) AS financial_sustainability_ratio,
    -- Step 2: Identify financial crisis risks based on Knowledge ID 27
    CASE
        WHEN f.fundsutilpct > 80 
             AND f.fundingstate = 'Critical' 
             AND ((f.donorcommitmentsusd / f.budgetallotusd) * (1 - f.fundsutilpct / 100) - (f.resourcegapsusd / f.budgetallotusd)) < 0.2 
        THEN 'Financial Crisis Risk'
        ELSE 'Financially Stable'
    END AS financial_status
FROM
    financials f
JOIN
    operations o ON f.finopsref = o.opsregistry
JOIN
    disasterevents d ON f.findistref = d.distregistry
ORDER BY
    financial_sustainability_ratio;

-- Find all operations with 'Red' or 'Black' emerglevel and show their PER.
-- Knowledge used: Personnel Effectiveness Ratio (PER) [ID 13]
-- Step 1: Join operations, humanresources, and financials, calculate PER.
SELECT
  o.opsregistry,
  o.emerglevel,
  ROUND(
    (CAST(hr.staffingprofile->'personnel'->>'total' AS NUMERIC) /
     (f.personnelcostsusd / 10000.0)) *
    (CAST(hr.staffingprofile->'readiness'->>'availability_percent' AS NUMERIC) / 100.0),
  2) AS per
FROM operations o
JOIN humanresources hr ON o.opsregistry = hr.hropsref
JOIN financials f ON o.opsregistry = f.finopsref
WHERE o.emerglevel IN ('Red', 'Black');

-- Intent: Calculate Resource Distribution Equity (RDE) and identify inequitable distribution
-- Uses KB ID 31: Resource Distribution Equity (RDE) and KB ID 48: Resource Distribution Inequity
-- Advanced Functions: CTEs, CASE expressions, JSON functions, percentile calculations

WITH coordination_factor_mapping AS (
    -- Step 1: Map coordination effectiveness levels to factors for RDE calculation
    SELECT
        b.beneregistry,
        b.benedistref,
        b.beneopsref,
        b.distequityidx,
        c.coordeffectlvl,
        CASE
            WHEN c.coordeffectlvl = 'High' THEN 1.2
            WHEN c.coordeffectlvl = 'Medium' THEN 1.0
            WHEN c.coordeffectlvl = 'Low' THEN 0.8
            ELSE 0
        END AS coordeffect_factor
    FROM
        beneficiariesandassessments b
    JOIN
        coordinationandevaluation c ON b.benedistref = c.coorddistref AND b.beneopsref = c.coordopsref
),
rde_calculation AS (
    -- Step 2: Calculate RDE using the formula from Knowledge ID 31
    SELECT
        cf.beneregistry,
        cf.benedistref,
        cf.beneopsref,
        cf.distequityidx,
        cf.coordeffectlvl,
        cf.coordeffect_factor,
        t.distributionpoints,
        t.deliverysuccessrate,
        ROUND(
            cf.distequityidx * 
            (1 + (t.distributionpoints / 20.0)) * 
            (1 - ((100 - t.deliverysuccessrate) / 100.0)) * 
            cf.coordeffect_factor,
        2) AS rde
    FROM
        coordination_factor_mapping cf
    JOIN
        transportation t ON cf.benedistref = t.transportdistref
)
-- Step 3: Retrieve final results with inequity identification and percentile calculation
SELECT
    r.beneregistry,
    r.rde,
    CASE
        WHEN r.rde < 0.4 
             AND r.distequityidx < 0.5 
             AND r.distributionpoints < 5
        THEN 'Resource Distribution Inequity'
        ELSE 'Normal Distribution'
    END AS distribution_status,
    d.impactmetrics->'population'->>'affected' AS affected_population
FROM
    rde_calculation r
JOIN
    disasterevents d ON r.benedistref = d.distregistry
ORDER BY
    r.rde;

-- Intent: Update operation status based on Logistics Performance Metric (LPM)
-- Knowledge used: #15 Logistics Performance Metric (LPM)
-- Advanced features: CTE, CASE expressions, multi-table joins, type casting

WITH logistics_performance AS (
    -- Step 1: Calculate the Logistics Performance Metric (LPM) for each operation
    -- LPM formula: (total delivery tons/hub capacity) * delivery success rate * (1-vehicle break rate/100) * 100
    SELECT 
        o.opsregistry,
        (t.totaldeliverytons / h.hubcaptons) * t.deliverysuccessrate * 
        (1 - (t.vehiclebreakrate / 100)) * 100 AS lpm_score
    FROM operations o
    JOIN distributionhubs h ON o.opshubref = h.hubregistry
    JOIN transportation t ON t.transporthubref = h.hubregistry AND t.transportdistref = o.opsdistref
)
-- Step 2: Update operation status based on LPM score
-- High LPM (>80) indicates operations can be completed, medium LPM (50-80) suggests scaling down,
-- low LPM (<50) means operations should remain active
UPDATE operations o
SET opsstatus = CASE 
        WHEN lp.lpm_score > 80 THEN 'Completed'::opsstatus_enum
        WHEN lp.lpm_score BETWEEN 50 AND 80 THEN 'Scaling Down'::opsstatus_enum
        ELSE 'Active'::opsstatus_enum
    END
FROM logistics_performance lp
WHERE o.opsregistry = lp.opsregistry
RETURNING o.opsregistry, lp.lpm_score, o.opsstatus;
test_cases:

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):

    verification_sql_active = """
    SELECT o.opsregistry, o.opsstatus
    FROM operations o
    JOIN (
        WITH logistics_performance AS (
            SELECT
                o.opsregistry,
                (t.totaldeliverytons / h.hubcaptons) * t.deliverysuccessrate *
                (1 - (t.vehiclebreakrate / 100)) * 100 AS lpm_score
            FROM operations o
            JOIN distributionhubs h ON o.opshubref = h.hubregistry
            JOIN transportation t ON t.transporthubref = h.hubregistry AND t.transportdistref = o.opsdistref
        )
        SELECT opsregistry
        FROM logistics_performance
        WHERE lpm_score < 50
    ) AS low_lpm_ops ON o.opsregistry = low_lpm_ops.opsregistry;

        assert status == 'Active', f"Operation {op_id} with LPM < 50 has status '{status}' instead of 'Active'."

-- Intent: Create a materialized view for environmental sustainability analysis
-- Knowledge used: #16 Environmental Impact Factor (EIF), #51 Environmental Impact Classification
-- Advanced features: Materialized view, CASE expression

CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW environmental_impact_analysis AS
SELECT 
    d.distregistry,
    d.haztype,
    e.envhealthregistry,
    -- Calculate EIF and round to 2 decimal places
    ROUND(
        (e.carbontons * (1 - (e.renewenergypct / 100)) + (100 - e.recyclepct) * 0.5), 
        2
    ) AS eif_score,
    -- Apply classification from KB #51
    CASE 
        WHEN (e.carbontons * (1 - (e.renewenergypct / 100)) + (100 - e.recyclepct) * 0.5) < 50 
        THEN 'Sustainable'
        WHEN (e.carbontons * (1 - (e.renewenergypct / 100)) + (100 - e.recyclepct) * 0.5) BETWEEN 50 AND 100 
        THEN 'Moderate Impact'
        ELSE 'High Impact'
    END AS environmental_impact_category
FROM 
    disasterevents d
    JOIN environmentandhealth e ON d.distregistry = e.envdistref
WITH DATA;

-- Intent: Archive and delete outdated coordination records
-- Knowledge used: #39 Cross-Agency Coordination Index (CACI)
-- Advanced features: Transaction control, string functions, array length

BEGIN;
-- Create table of records to be deleted
CREATE TABLE archived_coordination AS
SELECT 
    ce.*,
    -- Calculate CACI from KB #39
    (array_length(string_to_array(ce.partnerorgs, ';'), 1) / 10.0) * 
    CASE 
        WHEN ce.coordeffectlvl = 'High' THEN 3
        WHEN ce.coordeffectlvl = 'Medium' THEN 2
        ELSE 1
    END AS caci_score
FROM 
    coordinationandevaluation ce
    JOIN operations o ON ce.coordopsref = o.opsregistry;
-- Delete the archived records
DELETE FROM coordinationandevaluation
WHERE coordevalregistry IN (SELECT coordevalregistry FROM archived_coordination);
COMMIT;

-- Intent: Update supply inventory using JSONB operations
-- Knowledge used: #15 Logistics Performance Metric (LPM)
-- Advanced features: JSONB manipulation, lateral join

UPDATE supplies s
SET resourceinventory = jsonb_set(
    jsonb_set(
        s.resourceinventory,
        '{power, generators}', 
        (coalesce((s.resourceinventory->'power'->>'generators')::int, 0) + 20)::text::jsonb
    ),
    '{essentials, water_liters}', 
    (coalesce((s.resourceinventory->'essentials'->>'water_liters')::decimal, 0) + 5000)::text::jsonb
)
FROM transportation t
JOIN distributionhubs h ON t.transporthubref = h.hubregistry
WHERE 
    s.supplyregistry = t.transportsupref
    -- Calculate LPM (KB #15) and update only for low-performing logistics
    AND (t.totaldeliverytons / h.hubcaptons) * t.deliverysuccessrate * 
        (1 - (t.vehiclebreakrate / 100)) * 100 < 500;

-- Intent: Create a new type for resource utilization classification
-- Knowledge used: #50 Resource Utilization Classification
-- Advanced features: Type creation, ALTER TABLE, CHECK constraint

-- Create a new enum type based on KB #50
CREATE TYPE resource_utilization_class AS ENUM (
    'High Utilization', 
    'Moderate Utilization', 
    'Low Utilization'
);

-- Add a new column to store the classification
ALTER TABLE distributionhubs 
ADD COLUMN utilization_class resource_utilization_class;

-- Update the column using a DO block
DO $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE distributionhubs
    SET utilization_class = 
        CASE 
            WHEN (hubutilpct / 100) * (storecapm3 / storeavailm3) > 5 THEN 'High Utilization'::resource_utilization_class
            WHEN (hubutilpct / 100) * (storecapm3 / storeavailm3) BETWEEN 2 AND 5 THEN 'Moderate Utilization'::resource_utilization_class
            ELSE 'Low Utilization'::resource_utilization_class
        END;
END $$;
test_cases:
